title: VA / SEO Content Writer & Reporting — Local SEO & Google Business Profile (Remote, Full-Time)
description:
About Us

We're a UK-based digital agency working with trade businesses across the country — roofers, landscapers, electricians, plumbers, and more. We run productised local SEO services that help these businesses get found on Google and win more local customers. Our remote team is small, friendly, and focused — we care about doing great work without the corporate nonsense. If that sounds like your kind of setup, read on.



The Role

We need a reliable, detail-driven content writer and reporting assistant who understands local SEO and can produce consistent, high-quality content and clear client reports for our UK trade business clients. You'll be writing content that real homeowners and property managers read — so it needs to sound natural and rank well. You'll also be pulling together monthly performance reports that help our clients understand what's working. Briefs and templates are provided — your job is to execute them well.



What You'll Be Doing

Content Writing

-  Writing weekly Google Business Profile (GBP) posts for trade business clients (updates, offers, seasonal content)

-  Creating location-specific service pages (e.g. "Emergency Plumber in Guildford")

-  Writing meta titles, descriptions, and on-page SEO content

-  Crafting service area pages, FAQs, and "About" content

-  Drafting SEO-optimised review responses for Google

-  Writing blog posts for trade business websites (typically 600–1,000 words)

-  Following per-client brand voice guidelines — each client has their own tone



Client Reporting



-  Compiling monthly SEO reports for clients using our templates

-  Pulling GBP insights and stats (views, calls, direction requests, photo performance)

-  Summarising Google Analytics data (traffic, sessions, key pages)

-  Updating rank tracking reports and noting movement on target keywords

-  Flagging anything notable — big wins, drops, or things that need attention

-  Keeping Airtable up to date with content progress and reporting status



What We're Looking For



-  Strong English copywriting — British English spelling and phrasing is a must

-  Solid understanding of local SEO basics: NAP consistency, GBP, location relevance

-  Familiarity with Google Business Profile — what it is, why posts matter, and how to read the insights

-  Comfortable reading and summarising Google Analytics data (GA4)

-  Ability to write in multiple brand voices without sounding generic

-  Comfortable working from templates and structured briefs

-  Consistent availability and a reliable internet connection

-  Bonus: experience writing for trade or home improvement businesses

-  Bonus: familiarity with WordPress, SurferSEO, SEMrush, or Ahrefs



What You DON'T Need



-  You don't need to be a technical SEO expert

-  You don't need to run ads or manage campaigns

-  You don't need to build dashboards or code anything — templates are provided



Tools & Systems



-  WordPress — for uploading and publishing content (training provided)

-  Airtable — content tracking, brief management, and reporting status

-  Google Analytics (GA4) — for pulling traffic summaries

-  Google Business Profile — for posts and monthly insights

-  Google Search Console — for rank and visibility data

-  Google Docs / Sheets — day-to-day collaboration and report templates

-  SEMrush or Ahrefs or SEO Tools — for rank tracking exports (training provided)

-  Canva — occasional basic image tasks

-  Grammarly or similar — quality checking before submission



Working Arrangements



-  Full-time, Monday to Friday

-  Remote / work from home

-  Must overlap at least 3–4 hours per day with UK business hours (GMT/BST)

-  Weekly check-in and progress report required

-  Hourly rate: $5 USD depending on experience

-  Paid weekly



We look after the people who deliver well. There's real opportunity here to grow into a senior content role, take on more clients, and earn performance-based raises and bonuses over time.



How to Apply

To show you've read this carefully, please include the word "Localised" at the top of your application.



Send us:



A short intro message and your CV/résumé

Your answer to this question in 2–3 sentences: What is a Google Business Profile post, and why does it matter for local SEO?



We use AI tools in parts of our workflow, but we need to know you can write well independently. Please write your application intro in your own words — no AI assistants.



Thanks,

David

title: Trilingual Translator (Filipino/English/Chinese)
description:
Job Description: Trilingual Translator (Filipino/English/Chinese)



Job Title: Trilingual Translator & Interpreter

Location: [City/Remote/Hybrid]

Employment Type: Full-Time / Freelance / Contract

Reports To: [e.g., Localization Manager / Operations Director]



NOTE: THE CANDIDATE MUST BE PROFICIENT IN BOTH ORAL AND WRITTEN CHINESE.



Role Overview



We are seeking a highly skilled Trilingual Translator to facilitate communication across Tagalog (Filipino), English, and Chinese (Mandarin and Cantonese). The ideal candidate will act as a linguistic and cultural bridge, ensuring that technical documents, marketing materials, and verbal communications are accurately conveyed across these languages.



Key Responsibilities



Translation & Localization (Written)



· Translate a variety of documents from English to Filipino (Tagalog), English to Chinese (Simplified/Traditional), and vice versa.

· Localize marketing content to resonate with Filipino and Chinese-speaking audiences, adapting idioms and cultural references appropriately.

· Proofread and edit translations produced by other team members or automated tools to ensure accuracy, consistency, and brand voice.



Interpretation (Verbal)



· Provide real-time interpretation during business meetings, conference calls, and site visits between Filipino, English, Mandarin, and Cantonese speakers.

· Handle consecutive or simultaneous interpretation as required for negotiations or training sessions.



Cultural Consulting



· Advise management on cultural nuances and etiquette when dealing with Filipino and Chinese counterparts.

· Flag potential cultural misunderstandings in communications or business practices.



Cantonese Specialization



· Specifically manage interpretation/translation for Cantonese-speaking stakeholders, ensuring clarity between Cantonese and Mandarin documents/audio when required.



Qualifications







Must-Have Requirement



Proficient in oral and written Chinese is a must. 



Candidates must demonstrate full professional proficiency (oral) in Mandarin or Cantonese.



Candidates must demonstrate full professional proficiency (written) in Chinese.



Education & Experience



· Bachelor’s degree in Linguistics, Translation, Communications, or a related field (or equivalent work experience).

· Minimum [2-3] years of professional translation/interpretation experience.

· Native or near-native proficiency in Tagalog and English.

· Professional working proficiency in Mandarin (Simplified) and Cantonese.



Skills & Competencies



· Linguistic Excellence: Deep understanding of grammar, syntax, and idioms in all four language variants.

· Dialect Awareness: Must be able to distinguish between and accurately translate Cantonese and Mandarin where they diverge.

· Technical Proficiency: Familiarity with CAT tools (e.g., Trados, MemoQ) is a plus. Proficiency in MS Office and Google Workspace.

· Soft Skills: Excellent active listening, attention to detail, and the ability to remain calm under pressure (especially during live interpretation).

· Cultural Sensitivity: Deep understanding of Philippine, Chinese, and Western business etiquette.



Preferred (Nice to Have)



· Experience in Legal and Finance 

· Certification from a recognized translation/interpretation body.

· Experience with subtitling or voice-over coordination

title: AI UGC Video Editor (Direct Response Ads)
description:

title: Senior Amazon PPC Manager – Remote (Full-Time)
description:
Senior Amazon PPC Manager – Remote (Full-Time)



You live and breathe Amazon Ads. You've scaled accounts from 6 to 7 and 8 figures. You don't just "manage PPC" – you build strategies that crush the competition.



If that sounds like you, keep reading. If not, this role is not for you.



About Us

We are Fox Performance, an Amazon PPC agency founded in 2018. We work mainly with German clients, but also serve international brands across Europe and the USA. Our clients come from every industry you can imagine – supplements, beauty, home & living, pet, electronics, fashion, baby products, and more.



We are obsessed with Amazon. We test everything, read every update, break down every new beta feature, and push our clients to results they didn't think were possible. No autopilot. No "set and forget". No agency BS.

Now we are looking for a Senior Amazon PPC Manager – someone who can take full ownership of a portfolio of accounts, think strategically, and mentor junior tea
mbers along the way.



Who We Are NOT Looking For

Let's be direct. Do not apply if:



You are looking for a short-term gig or just a "bridge job" until something else comes up.

You are working multiple jobs in parallel and trying to squeeze us in on the side. We need your focus. Period.

You don't want to grow, learn, and push yourself every single week.

You want to clock in, clock out and never go beyond the bare minimum.

You see Amazon PPC as "just a job" instead of a craft you want to master.

You can't take direct, honest feedback without taking it personally.



We're not for everyone – and that's okay.

Who We ARE Looking For

We are looking for a true A-Player. Someone who:



Wants to go full throttle and be one of the best in the game.

Treats client accounts like their own business.

Is hungry, curious, and obsessed with getting better.

Wants to grow with us long-term and build a real career.

Brings energy, ideas, and ownership to the table every single day.



If that's you – we'd love to meet you.



What You'll Own



Full Account Ownership: manage a portfolio of premium client accounts end-to-end – from strategy to execution to client communication.

Advertising Strategy: build PPC strategies across Sponsored Products, Sponsored Brands, Sponsored Display – and where relevant, Amazon DSP and AMC.

Launch & Scale: from new product launches to aggressive scaling of existing winners – you know when to push and when to defend.

Advanced Optimization: bid strategies, placement optimization, SKU-level profitability, TACoS management, dayparting, portfolio structuring – you go way beyond dashboard-level.

Account Audits & Strategic Planning: diagnose weaknesses, identify opportunities, build 30/60/90 day roadmaps for every account.

Client Communication: lead client calls, present results, push back when needed, be the trusted expert clients rely on.

Mentorship: help our Junior PPC Managers and Assistants grow – share your playbook, review their work, develop the next generation.

Process Development: help us build better SOPs, templates and internal tools – your experience shapes how we operate.

Stay Ahead: test new Amazon features, evaluate new tools, and bring fresh ideas to the team.





What You Must Know (Non-Negotiables)

These are the things you absolutely need to have under your belt as a Senior:



Deep mastery of all Sponsored Ads formats: Sponsored Products, Sponsored Brands (incl. Video & Brand Store), Sponsored Display (incl. Audiences & Custom Creatives), and how they interact.

Campaign architecture: SKAG, single-keyword, theme-based, performance-based – you know which structure works when and why.

Advanced bidding strategies: dynamic bids, placement modifiers, top-of-search optimization, bid by placement, bid hijacking defense.

Keyword research & targeting: Helium 10 (Cerebro, Magnet, Frankenstein), Brand Analytics, Search Query Performance Report (SQP) – you can pull insights most managers miss.

TACoS vs ACoS: you understand the difference, when to optimize for which, and how to communicate it to clients.

Search Term Reports & Negative Mining: you build clean, performance-focused accounts without keyword cannibalization.

Brand Defense & Conquesting: you protect your client's branded search and attack competitors strategically.

Organic & Paid Synergy: you understand how PPC influences organic ranking (and vice versa) – BSR, indexation, halo effect.

Listing Fundamentals: you know that even the best PPC can't fix a bad listing – and you can identify what needs to change.

Reporting & Storytelling: you don't just deliver numbers – you tell the story behind them.

Bulk File Operations: you can edit, optimize and upload bulk files like a surgeon.

Tool Stack: Helium 10, Brand Analytics, Pacvue / Perpetua / Adtomic / Sellerboard or similar – at least one professional ad management tool you've worked with hands-on.

Bonus (high value): Amazon DSP, AMC (Amazon Marketing Cloud), Vendor Central, international marketplaces (US, UK, DE, FR, IT, ES).



If you read this list and thought "yes, that's me" – continue. If half of these are unfamiliar, this role is not the right fit yet.



What You Bring



4+ years of hands-on Amazon Advertising experience (agency or in-house), ideally managing multiple 6- and 7-figure accounts.

Proven track record of scaling Amazon accounts profitably – with real case studies you can walk us through.

Expert-level data analysis skills – Excel / Google Sheets is your playground; pivot tables, XLOOKUP, conditional logic are second nature.

Strategic mindset – you think in frameworks, not just dashboards.

Excellent English (spoken and written). German is a big plus, but not required.

Strong communication – confident in client calls, clear in writing, direct but professional.

Ownership mentality – fully accountable, no excuses.

Stable internet and a professional remote work setup.





What We Offer

You already know the foundation we offer (100% remote, MacBook, full-time, long-term, competitive USD salary, after one year: 13th month pay, 20 days PTO, paid sick leave, Philippine holidays...).





Ownership & Growth



Clear path to Team Lead / Head of PPC – we are growing, and senior hires are who we promote from.

Voice in strategy – you help shape how the agency operates.

Direct line to leadership – no middle management, no politics.

Conference & event budget – Amazon Unboxed, PPC events, industry conferences – we send you.

Annual learning budget – courses, certifications, books, coaches – you choose.



Time & Space to Do Great Work



Flexible hours – core overlap with German business hours (2–3 hours/day), the rest is yours.

Deep Work culture – no unnecessary meetings, no micromanagement.

Real team culture – weekly team calls, virtual coffee, and annual team events.



If you read the full text start your message with "HI WOLF"

title: Video Editor + Graphic Designer (AI-Fluent) — Remote, Full-Time
description:
Hi, we're Real Mad Honey. We sell rare honey from the Himalayas and Turkey that actually does something you can feel. Our Meta ads run across the US, UK and EU, and we're scaling hard in 2026. That means we need more creative, faster, without the quality dropping.

That's where you come in.



Who you'll work with:

You'll be reporting directly to our Creative Strategist. They own the creative direction, the briefs, the angles, the hooks, and the testing roadmap. Your job is to take their briefs and turn them into finished ads and assets. You'll have a clear brief for every piece of work, regular feedback loops, and a direct line to ask questions when something isn't clear. If you've worked in a setup where the strategist and the editor push each other to make the work sharper, you'll feel at home.



What you'll actually be doing day to day:

Most of your week is editing ad videos. Think UGC creators filming selfie-style, vox pop / street interview footage , and VSL-style longer explainer videos. The Creative Strategist will hand you raw footage, scripts and briefs. You turn it into scroll-stopping ads with clean subtitles, good pacing, sound design that doesn't overwhelm, and hooks that earn the first three seconds.

On top of that, you'll design statics. Ad statics first, then occasional website visuals and landing page sections. Figma is our tool. If you already think in components and design tokens, even better.

You'll also build organic assets for our social channels. Reels edits, carousel posts, story templates. The organic stuff doesn't have to sell, it has to feel like the brand.



The AI part is not optional:

Honestly, this is the thing that separates the editors we want from the ones we don't. We use AI throughout the workflow. Claude for scripting, copy, ideation, briefing. AI tools for transcription, subtitle generation, rough cuts, upscaling, voice cleanup, background removal, thumbnail variants, image generation for moodboards and concepts. If you can cut a 30-second UGC ad in 45 minutes instead of 3 hours because you know which AI tools to stack, you'll do great here. If AI tools scare you or you're still doing everything manually in 2026, this role will frustrate you.



The systems we use:

Trello for task management and deliverable tracking. Claude AI for briefs, scripts, brainstorms, copy. Figma for design. For editing, we work in the Adobe suite (Premiere Pro, After Effects, Photoshop, Illustrator), so strong Adobe skills are important. If you also use CapCut Pro or DaVinci for certain cuts, that's fine as long as the output is clean and deliverables come back in the right format. Google Drive for asset handoff.



What we need from you as a person:

Communication is honestly half the job. If a deadline is going to slip, we want to know on day one, not day four. If a brief from the strategist is unclear, ask. If you think the script is weak, say so. We'd rather push back on a brief than receive a polished ad that misses.

You'll be writing Trello updates, commenting on review links, jumping on the occasional Loom with the Creative Strategist, and responding to Slack-style messages during overlap hours. You don't need perfect English but you need to be clear, direct, and reliable. "Done by Friday" has to mean done by Friday.



Who this role is for:

Someone who's been editing performance ads for DTC brands for at least 2 to 3 years. You've seen UGC. You've seen vox pop. You've cut VSLs. You understand that an ad is not a film, it's a sales pitch with 3 seconds to land. You're fluent in Adobe Premiere and ideally comfortable in After Effects for motion graphics and text animations. You've used Figma seriously, not just opened it once. You've integrated AI into your pipeline because you care about speed without losing taste.



Who this role is not for:

Editors who take three days to turn around a simple ad cut. People who wait to be told exactly what to do. Designers who make beautiful work that doesn't convert. Anyone who needs hand-holding on Trello tickets or forgets to respond for two days.



The work schedule

Full-time, 40 hours per week. We're based in Europe so ideally you can overlap 3 to 4 hours with CET working hours. The rest is async.



How to apply:

Send us three things.

- First, your portfolio. We want to see ad videos you've edited (UGC, vox pop, VSL if you have them) and static ads you've designed. If it's behind a password, include it.



- Second, a short Loom (2 to 4 minutes) where you walk us through one ad you're proud of. Tell us why you made the choices you made. We care more about your thinking than your reel.



-Third, tell us which AI tools you actually use in your workflow and what you use them for. One paragraph is fine. If you just say "ChatGPT" we'll know.



Looking forward to seeing your work.

title: Meta Ads Creative Researcher & Strategist - Dropshipping Company (Funnelish)
description:
About the role

We're a fast-growing dropshipping brand selling beauty and health products in the UK and Australian markets, targeting a 40+ demographic. Our ads run on Meta and are built around advertorials and sales funnels (Funnelish). We're looking for a Creative Strategist who keeps our ad pipeline full — sourcing competitor inspiration, generating fresh angles, and producing new creative variants using AI tools.



This is a research and production role. You'll work closely with our creative editor and funnel builders to make sure every ad we test aligns with our landers, ad copy, and audience.



What you'll do

- Research and collect competitor ad creatives daily via Trendtrack, the Meta Ads Library, and other ad spy tools

- Analyse swipes structurally: identify hooks, angles, psychological triggers, and funnel alignment — not just save them

- Build a well-organised swipe library so our creative editor always has ready-to-adapt material

- Use AI tools (e.g. ChatGPT, Claude, Midjourney, CapCut AI) to generate new creative variants based on winning ads

- Adapt visual elements and hook/angle copy in creatives to create fresh ad variations for testing

- Ensure every output aligns with our advertorial tone, sales page, ad text, and 40+ target audience

- Deliver output in a structured format our team can brief directly to the creative editor or feed into our launching workflow



What we're looking for

- Proven experience with Meta Ads — you understand what makes a direct-response ad work

- Hands-on experience with ad intelligence tools (Trendtrack, Meta Ads Library, Minea, BigSpy, or similar)

- Solid understanding of hooks, angles, and how they connect to a landing page or advertorial

- Comfortable using AI tools for both visual adaptation and copy generation

- Familiarity with advertorial-style funnels and direct-response copywriting principles

- Strong eye for detail — you understand the difference between a swipe and a copy-paste

- Experience in beauty, health, or wellness e-commerce is a strong plus

- Able to work independently and manage your own output without micromanagement



Skills & tools

Meta Ads Library, Trendtrack / ChatGPT, Claude / AI image creating tools / Hook & angle writing / Direct-response copywriting / Advertorial funnels / Creative briefing



What to include in your application

- A short overview of your experience with ad research and creative strategy

- Examples of swipe analysis you've done — show us how you deconstruct an ad, not just collect it

- Any AI-generated creative variants or adapted ads you've produced

- Tools you use regularly and your level of proficiency

- Your availability and rate (per hour or per deliverable)

title: Video Editor
description:
We're looking for a talented, fast-moving Video Editor who wants to grow long-term with our team as we scale digital ecommerce and SaaS businesses. You will spend 100% of your time editing direct response video ads for Meta, working closely with our Creative Strategist, UGC creators, and the broader team to produce ads that convert.



We've been building in SaaS and ecommerce for 5+ years, and we are now doubling down on digital products, starting with 
 and several additional projects currently in development.



If you love editing high-performing direct response ads, experimenting with new styles and pacing, and pushing creative boundaries, this role is for you.



ABOUT THIS ROLE:

You will be responsible for editing direct response video ads end-to-end, primarily for Meta. You'll work from our large in-house UGC library, including product b-roll, non-product b-roll, and footage from multiple UGC creators, and turn it into scroll-stopping ads with strong hooks, tight pacing, and clear conversion-focused storytelling. You'll also have the freedom to mix in stock clips, screen recordings, and AI-generated content where it fits.



You will work directly with our Creative Strategist, following briefs and scripts precisely, but you're also expected to bring your own ideas to the table, new edit styles, new hook formats, new pacing experiments, and new ways to make our ads stand out.



THIS ROLE IS FOR SOMEONE WHO:

Lives and breathes video editing for direct response, understands what makes someone stop scrolling in the first 5 seconds, and obsesses over hooks, pacing, captions, sound design, and retention. You should be comfortable taking creative direction but also confident enough to suggest better ideas and experiment on your own.



RESPONSIBILITIES:

Your main focus will be editing direct response video ads daily, pulling from our UGC library, cutting footage, adding captions, sound effects, music, b-roll, motion graphics, and on-screen text to maximize hook strength and watch time. You'll follow scripts and briefs from our Creative Strategist, then iterate quickly on variations, hooks, and angles for testing.



You'll also be open-minded about creating AI video hooks and full AI-driven video ads, experimenting with new tools and formats as the space evolves. We expect you to actively look for inspiration on x.com, study top-performing ads from competitors, and pull ideas from people in completely different industries to bring fresh editing styles and formats into our ads.



REQUIREMENTS:

You should have real experience editing direct response video ads, ideally for ecommerce brands, with a portfolio of ads you've cut that have actually run and performed. Strong skills in Premiere Pro / After Effects / Davinci Resolve are essential, along with a sharp eye for hooks, pacing, captions, and sound design. You should also be able to work fast, handle multiple variations per concept, and turn around edits quickly while staying organized when working with a large UGC library.



YOU SHOULD ALSO:

Be open-minded and eager to try new editing styles and AI tools, active on x.com and following ad creative trends, highly proactive and independent, and able to both follow a creative brief precisely and bring your own strong ideas.



HIGHLY PREFERRED EXPERIENCE:

Experience editing direct response ads for ecommerce brands, especially ads that have scaled on Meta. Big plus if you're skilled at producing realistic AI voiceovers with ElevenLabs. Even bigger plus if you can create full AI UGC videos and hooks with properly synced voice audio. Bonus if you've worked with UGC-heavy ad accounts at scale.



COMPENSATION:

Starting salary is $800/month based on experience and track record, with performance-based bonuses and long-term growth opportunities. Our goal is to build a small but extremely strong team that can scale multiple businesses together over the next several years.



HOW TO APPLY:

When applying, please include:

- Your experience editing direct response video ads

- A portfolio or examples of ads you've edited (ideally ones that ran on Meta)

- Tools you use (Premiere, After Effects, CapCut, etc.)

- Examples of hooks or editing styles you're proud of

- Any AI video, AI UGC, or ElevenLabs voiceover work you've done

- Results from ads you've edited (CTR, hold rate, conversion rates, etc.)

- Your experience working with UGC libraries and Creative Strategists



Applications without video examples may not be reviewed. If you're a serious Video Editor who wants to push boundaries in direct response ad creative, we'd love to hear from you.

RESPONSIBILITIES:

- Direct response media buying

- Subscription-based ecommerce or SaaS

- Meta Ads best practices and recent updates

- Scaling campaigns profitably



YOU SHOULD ALSO:

- Be open-minded and eager to learn

- Actively test new strategies and funnels

- Have strong analytical thinking



HIGHLY PREFERRED EXPERIENCE

Experience working with quiz-funnel based subscription brands, such as:

- BetterMe

- Hint

- Nebula

- Simple Life App

- Reverse Health

- Liven

- Performance-based bonuses

- Long-term growth opportunities

HOW TO APPLY

- Your experience with subscription-based businesses

- Examples of ad creatives or campaigns you’ve worked on



Applications without specific performance metrics may not be reviewed. If you’re a serious direct response media buyer who wants to scale quiz funnel subscription brands, we’d love to hear from you.

title: FACEBOOK ADS INFRASTRUCTURE VA - IMMEDIATE START
description:
FACEBOOK ADS INFRASTRUCTURE VA - IMMEDIATE START



We run paid media operations across multiple markets in Europe.
 is strict and profiles go down fast — we need someone who knows this world, can move quickly, and keeps our infrastructure healthy at all times.



We're scaling fast. A new market drops every few weeks and we need someone who can keep up.



This is a long-term role with a lot of work ahead. You'll start with one setup this week and grow from there as we expand.



---



**Scope of work:**



Immediately this week we need a full market setup completed. A setup means: 2 Business Managers, 5–6
 profiles per BM, ad accounts, pages, pixels, and proxies all connected and ready to run ads.



In 2–3 weeks, another full market setup. Then a new market setup roughly every 3 weeks from there.



Between setups, you're responsible for keeping everything in tip top shape — monitoring profiles, coordinating with the asset provider when profiles go down, replacing them, and refreshing proxies. The BMs need to always be healthy and ready.



This is 10–20 hrs/week with volume that only grows.



---



**What you'll be doing:**



- Setting up Business Managers, ad accounts, and
 profiles to a layout we provide

- Connecting proxies and anti-detect browser profiles (AdsPower)

- Warming up profiles and hooking everything up to the BM structure

- Coordinating with asset providers — finding best price, flagging us to send payment

- Following up with providers when profiles have issues, get banned, or need replacing

- Refreshing and swapping proxies when profiles go down

- Connecting pages, pixels, and datasets to the correct accounts

- Handling verifications, checkpoints, and the usual bugs that come with this work

- Keeping everything organized and reporting clearly when something needs attention



---



**To apply, tell us specifically:**



- What BM, profile, and page management work you've done — exact detail, not general

- Campaigns you've worked on and what your actual role was

- Whether you've dealt with asset providers and how

- Your experience with proxies — which ones, how you set them up

- Anti-detect browsers you've used (AdsPower, Multilogin, Dolphin, etc.)

- How you've handled profile verifications, bans, and replacements

- Any other relevant infrastructure work in paid media



Send a full overview of what you've actually done. We're not looking for a resume — we want to see you've been in the trenches.



If you're serious, reply with **LETS GET AFTER IT** at the top of your message.



Work starts this week.

title: CRM Integration And Automation Specialist
description:

n8n (or similar advanced automation tools)

Troubleshoot and resolve workflow issues quickly

Continuously improve system efficiency and performance



Do Not Apply If you dont have strong experience with these tools

Message directly on WhatsApp:

A brief introduction

title: Graphic Designer
description:
We are hiring a full-time Social Media Stories and Graphic Designer to join our growing marketing company. This role focuses on creating Instagram stories, thumbnails, and feed graphics for multiple brands, including med spas, plastic surgeons, hair restoration clinics, dermatology practices, real estate brands, barbers, nail technicians, and more.

This position is not focused on heavy video editing. The priority is strong visual design, creativity, and the ability to consistently produce high-quality content at scale.



Responsibilities

-Create approximately 30 Instagram stories per day across multiple client accounts

-Design Instagram stories, thumbnails, and feed graphics

-Use AI tools to generate and enhance images is a must 

-Design primarily using Canva and/or Photoshop

-Light video work such as simple story animations or text overlays

-Follow brand guidelines while keeping content clean, modern, and engaging

-Manage multiple brands while meeting daily deadlines



Requirements

-Strong graphic design skills

-Advanced experience using AI image generation tools

-Proficient in Canva and/or Photoshop

-Strong understanding of Instagram layouts, trends, and visual storytelling

-Ability to work efficiently under daily production demands

-Creative, reliable, and detail-oriented

-Must be able to work on Pacific Standard Time (PST) schedule



Position Details

-Full-time role

-40 hours per week

-Monday through Friday

-9:00 AM – 5:00 PM PST

-Monthly Pay: $300

-Long-term position with strong growth opportunities as the company scales



How to Apply

To be considered, your first message must include:

-Your portfolio

-Examples of Instagram stories, thumbnails, or feed posts

-A brief explanation of your experience with AI image generation

-A short paragraph explaining why you would be a great fit for this role and how you see yourself growing with our company



?? Applications that do not include all of the above will not be reviewed.

title: Interior Designer (Full-Time, Remote) – Build Magical Spaces for a Global Hospitality Brand
description:
We’re looking for a full-time Interior Designer to join a fast-growing hospitality brand that builds immersive, story-driven spaces, often described as the Disney of short stays.



This is not a freelance or side-hustle role.



This is a full-time, exclusive position for a designer who wants to fully commit to one brand, own projects end-to-end, and build a long-term creative career.



If you’re looking to juggle multiple clients or keep other professional commitments, this role will not be the right fit.





About the Role:



You will be the sole Interior Designer responsible for running design projects from start to finish.



That means you will:



- Lead each project from concept to final delivery

- Drive timelines, milestones, and 
ntum

- Run kick-offs, reviews, and design sessions

- Own modelling, layouts, FF&E, and specifications

- Proactively push projects forward without being chased

- Collaborate with a creative director for vision and alignment



You won’t be given tasks.

You’ll be given ownership.





Exclusivity & Focus (Important):



This role requires full professional focus.





We’re looking for someone who:



- Treats this as their only professional design role

- Does not take on other client work or employment

- Wants to invest their creative energy into building one brand deeply

- Thinks and operates as if this were their own business



We believe the quality and pace of work we’re aiming for is only possible with complete focus and commitment.





The Kind of Designer We’re Looking For



This role is for someone who:

- Thinks like an owner, not an employee

- Is highly proactive and self-driven

- Is comfortable being the single point of responsibility

- Is responsive and reliable in fast-moving environments

- Takes pride in their work and their craft

- Wants to build a long-term career, not just complete jobs



We care far more about ownership, judgement, and 
ntum than about where you studied or how many years you’ve worked.



How We Work:



- Fully remote

- Outcome-driven

- High autonomy, high trust

- Fast-moving projects

- Real creative freedom



The only strict expectation is responsiveness when tagged or messaged, because that’s how projects keep moving.





Compensation Structure



- Monthly base pay

- Plus a performance-based bonus per project onboarded



High performers are directly rewarded for the projects they successfully deliver.



Exact figures will be discussed with shortlisted candidates.





What Success Looks Like



In your first 90 days, you’ll be:



- Running multiple projects independently

- Driving timelines without being prompted

- Producing high-quality, immersive designs

- Becoming the creative owner of our spaces





How to Apply



Please include:



- A link to your portfolio

- A short video answering these questions, uploaded to a public google drive link:



“This role requires full professional focus with no other work commitments. Why does that suit you at this stage of your career?” and "If someone was to tell you, you were going to join the Disney of the short-stay industry, how would that make you feel?"





If you’re looking for a place to truly own your craft and grow long-term, we’d love to hear from you.

title: Inventory & Operations VA (E-commerce, Multi-Market)
description:
We're an e-commerce brand selling across the US, UK, EU, Australia, and Canada. We need a reliable, detail-oriented VA to manage our inventory operations daily.



The main job:

Monitor our inventory spreadsheet every day, identify which products need to be reordered, create purchase orders, coordinate with suppliers and warehouses, and manage Amazon FBA replenishment across our markets.



Secondary tasks (small part of the role):

- Upload ad creatives to Meta Ads Manager following a simple naming guide

- Run a bi-weekly data export and analysis using a provided template



You're a great fit if you:

- Have experience in e-commerce operations, supply chain, or inventory management

- Are comfortable reading spreadsheet data and taking action from it

- Can write professional
s to suppliers and warehouses

- Are self-managed — we use Loom videos and written SOPs for training

- Have used tools like Google Sheets, Shopify, Shiphero, Amazon Seller Central, or Wise



To apply:

Send your resume and answer this question in 5–10 sentences:

"Describe a multi-step operational process you've managed regularly. What tools did you use and how did you make sure nothing fell through the cracks?"

title: Roofing Operations & Takeoff Specialist
description:
We are seeking a highly skilled and detail-oriented Virtual Assistant to support core business operations, with a primary focus on roofing takeoffs and estimating support.



This is not a basic admin role—we are looking for a proactive, high-level operator who can accurately interpret plans, perform material takeoffs, and support the estimating process, while also contributing to broader operational efficiency.

The ideal candidate has hands-on experience in roofing takeoffs, thrives in a fast-paced environment, and takes ownership of their work with an operator mindset.





Key Responsibilities:

1. Roofing Takeoffs & Estimating (Primary Focus)

* Perform accurate roofing takeoffs from plans, blueprints, and aerial measurement tools

* Calculate materials, labor estimates, and project scope details

* Work with platforms such as EagleView, Roofr, Hover, or similar tools

* Assist in preparing and organizing estimates and proposals

* Identify discrepancies or missing information in plans and flag proactively



2. Operations & Project Support

* Support project coordination and workflow tracking

* Maintain and update job files, documentation, and CRM systems

* Assist in creating and refining SOPs for estimating and operations

* Ensure all project-related data is organized and accessible



3. Administrative & Communication Support

* Manage
s, follow-ups, and internal communication

* Coordinate with tea
mbers, vendors, and subcontractors as needed

* Track tasks and ensure nothing falls through the cracks





Required Experience & Skills:

* Proven experience with roofing takeoffs (REQUIRED)

* Strong understanding of roofing systems, materials, and terminology

* Experience with takeoff and measurement tools (EagleView, Roofr, Hover, etc.)

* Excellent attention to detail and accuracy

* Strong written and verbal communication skills

* Highly organized with the ability to manage multiple projects

* Proactive, self-starter with an owner/operator mindset



Preferred (Not Required):

* Experience supporting a roofing company or contractor

* Familiarity with CRMs (JobNimbus, AccuLynx, or similar)

* Experience with estimating software (Xactimate or similar)

* Background in construction, estimating, or project management

* Experience building or maintaining SOPs



Success Profile:

* Takes full ownership of takeoffs and delivers with precision

* Thinks ahead and identifies issues before they become problems

* Operates with speed, accuracy, and reliability

* Communicates clearly and consistently with the team

* Constantly looks for ways to improve systems and processes





Summary:

This role is ideal for someone who is not just administrative, but who can step into the operational heartbeat of a roofing business, owning the takeoff and estimating support function while helping the company scale efficiently.





To fast-track your application, please submit the following information:

Resume: A link to your Google Drive. Please ensure the sharing setting is set to "Anyone with the link."



IMPORTANT REMINDER: To advance in the screening process, you must submit the Podio Form. Timely completion is essential to guarantee your application receives prompt attention.



[REQUIRED] Podio Form Link: 
/webforms/
/

title: High-Performance BDR – U.S. EdTech
description:
We are currently hiring 10 additional remote Business Development Representatives (BDRs) to handle outbound calling, qualify prospects, and book meetings. The 10 new members will join an existing team of 10 BDRs.



About Company

The company helps school districts turn student interest in gaming, digital media, and technology into career-connected learning pathways that improve engagement, build real skills, and support future-ready learning.



We work with K-12 school and district leaders across the U.S. and help them launch programs in areas like game design, digital media, and other future-focused pathways.



We are hiring strong Business Development Representatives to join our internal outbound team.



This is not a basic call center role. We are looking for BDRs who can speak strong English, think on their feet, ask smart follow-up questions, build interest with decision-makers, and consistently schedule qualified meetings.



What you will do

- Make outbound calls to school and district decision-makers in the U.S.

- Support outreach with
 and follow-up

- Speak with administrators, CTE leaders, curriculum leaders, and other education stakeholders

- Create interest and qualify potential opportunities

- Schedule qualified meetings for the Account Executive team

- Handle objections professionally

- Follow our sales process while sounding natural and confident

- Maintain strong activity, discipline, and follow-up quality



Who we are looking for

- Strong spoken and written English

- Confident live-call communication

- Experience in cold calling, appointment setting, SDR/BDR, lead generation, or outbound sales

- Ability to ask good follow-up questions instead of reading scripts blindly

- Professional, competitive, coachable, and accountable

- Able to work U.S.-aligned hours

- Stable internet and professional remote setup



Big plus

- Experience calling U.S. decision-makers

- Experience in B2B, SaaS, consultative sales, or education-related sales

- Strong objection handling

- Strong performance in KPI-driven environments



Compensation

- $5/hour base

- Bonuses for qualified meetings scheduled

- Higher bonus tiers based on performance thresholds

- Growth opportunity inside a serious internal sales team



Important

To be considered, all applicants must complete the application tasks. Tasks will bes sent over direct message after you apply.

title: Assistant Operations Manager - Remote| Singapore Company
description:
ASSISTANT OPERATIONS MANAGER — REMOTE | SINGAPORE COMPANY

Slim Sassy Strong Pte Ltd | Remote Full-Time | php20K–45K+ | Singapore travel opportunities



You’re good at your job. You’re just tired of the commute.

You’ve spent years working onsite — handling clients, managing workflows, keeping things running. You’re competent, you’re professional, and you show up.

But the traffic is costing you 2–3 hours every day. Hours you’d rather spend building something.

We’re offering you a way out of the commute — without stepping back in your career.



Slim Sassy Strong is a women’s wellness education company headquartered in Singapore. We are hiring one Assistant Operations Manager to join our remote operations team, with a structured path to Operations Manager and Senior Operations Manager.



This is a serious career role, not a work-from-home side gig.



|Phase   |Role                     |Compensation    |

|--------|-------------------------|----------------|

|Month 1 |Training                 |php20,000 fixed   |

|Phase 2 |AOM (KPI-based)          |php28,000 starting|

|Phase 3 |Operations Manager       |php35,000–php45,000 |

|Advanced|Senior Operations Manager|php45,000+        |



Plus: selected tea
mbers travel to Singapore for company events and operations.

Advancement is based on performance — not time served.



You will work directly with Singapore-based leadership as an operational extension of the business:

	-	Manage executive calendars and protect leadership time

	-	Own structured
 communication and client follow-ups

	-	Track KPIs, deadlines, and team task execution

	-	Conduct client-facing calls — professionally and confidently

	-	Support the consult pipeline and marketing-to-ops handover

	-	Maintain SOP compliance and operational documentation



As you grow into OM, you lead the team, own systems, and make decisions independently.

Client communication is a core part of this role — not an occasional task.



Why this works for you:

	-	No commute. Work from home, full-time, with a Singapore company on your resume.

	-	Real career track — AOM ? OM ? SOM with compensation that grows with your scope

	-	Singapore travel — top performers join us onsite for events and operational stints

	-	Above-market ceiling — ?45K+ for senior performers

	-	Long-term stability — we grow with people who perform; we don’t churn talent



You’ll do well here if:

You’ve worked onsite in operations, client coordination, admin, hospitality, or a similar field. You’re used to being relied on. You’re not looking for an easy job — you’re looking for a better setup to do your best work in.

Specifically, you:

	-	Are confident handling clients directly — calls, not just chat

	-	Stay composed and professional under pressure

	-	Meet deadlines without being chased

	-	Communicate clearly and directly, especially when escalating

	-	Are fast with digital tools and willing to learn new systems

	-	Want to build a career, not just collect a paycheck

	-	You don’t mind putting in extra hours when it counts. You just want those hours to actually mean something.



Technical requirements

	-	Minimum 50 WPM typing speed

	-	Strong written and spoken English — business standard

	-	Stable internet with a documented backup connection

	-	Own laptop suitable for business use

	-	Familiarity with Google Workspace or similar tools



How to apply:



 with the exact subject line:

Assistant Operations Manager Application

Include all of the following — incomplete applications will not be reviewed:

	1.	One paragraph: what you’ve handled onsite and why you’re ready to bring that to a remote leadership role

	2.	Your current city and power/internet stability details

	3.	Device specs + internet speed screenshot

	4.	Your backup plan during outages

	5.	A 1–2 minute informal audio introduction

	6.	DISC profile screenshot (OnlineJobs)

	7.	Typing test screenshot (min. 50 WPM)

title: ASSISTANT OPERATIONS MANAGER – CLIENT EXPERIENCE (Female only, Full-Time, Remote – Philippines)
description:
Join a mission-driven company empowering women across Asia to live stronger, healthier, and more confident lives.



We're expanding our operations team — and we're looking for a sharp, polished, and proactive Assistant Operations Manager to be the professional frontline between our clients and our Singapore leadership team.



This is a long-term, full-time position with real career growth for the right person.



YOUR ROLE

You'll work directly with our Operations Manager and Singapore-based Director, supporting the full client journey — from onboarding through to programme completion — while ensuring backend operations run accurately and on time.



Your main responsibilities:

> Manage client onboarding, scheduling, confirmations, and reminders per SOP

> Handle professional client communications via phone, Zoom, and messaging platforms

> Coordinate with Singapore-based coaches and leadership on session logistics and events

> Maintain accurate records across CRM systems, trackers, and shared drives

title: Graphic Designer (Document & Brand Design)
description:
NOTE: DO NOT APPLY IF YOU ARE A VA, OR SOCIAL MEDIA MANAGER. WE ARE LOOKING FOR A DEDICATED GRAPHIC DESIGNER.



-ABOUT US-



We're Pure Proposals, a PandaDoc consulting agency that helps businesses build professional proposal and quoting systems. We work with clients across Australia, the US, Canada, New Zealand, and the UK. Our clients range from entertainment venue groups to construction companies to SaaS businesses. We need a designer who can make their documents look incredible.



What You'll Be Designing



Document and proposal designs - this is the core of the role. We build proposal templates, quotes, contracts, and booking confirmations inside a platform called PandaDoc for our clients. You don't need to know PandaDoc. Your job is to design the layouts in Figma or your preferred design tool, and our team implements them into the platform. You'll take a client's brand and create polished, professional document designs that we then bring to life. What matters is your eye for layout, typography, and clean document design.



Social media graphics - LinkedIn infographics, carousels, and post graphics. We need someone who can take a topic or set of data points and turn them into visually compelling, shareable graphics. Not templated filler. Think the kind of infographic or carousel that makes someone stop scrolling because it's genuinely useful and looks great. We'll provide the content and direction, you make it visual.



Case studies and one-pagers - single or multi-page PDFs that showcase client results. Clean, modern layout with strong visual hierarchy.



Ebooks and guides - longer-form designed documents (10-20 pages) for lead magnets and client resources.

Website design assets - banners, graphics, and visual elements for our Framer website.



Brand collateral - helping consolidate and maintain our brand guidelines, creating consistent assets across all touchpoints.



What We're Looking For



Must-haves:



Strong portfolio showing document layout and design (proposals, pitch decks, reports, case studies, or similar). This is the most important thing. If your portfolio is all logos and social media posts, this isn't the right fit.



Clean, modern design aesthetic. Think rounded edges, bold but not overwhelming colours, strong typography, plenty of white space. We like designs that feel premium without being overly corporate.



Proficiency in Figma and/or Canva. Adobe suite is a bonus but not required.



Responsive communication. We move fast and need someone who replies within a few hours during working hours.

Comfortable working asynchronously with clear briefs via Slack.



Nice-to-haves:



Experience with infographic design or data visualization

Experience with presentation/pitch deck design

Basic web design skills (Framer, Webflow, or similar)



How to Apply

Send us:



1. A link to your portfolio. This is the most important part of your application. We're evaluating your design style and quality before anything else. Show us document layouts, proposal designs, pitch decks, case studies, brand collateral. The more relevant to what we described above, the better.



2. A short message (3-4 sentences max) telling us: your timezone, your hourly rate, and your typical availability per week.



3. One example from your portfolio that you think best represents the style of work we're describing.



We will not respond to applications without a portfolio link.



Put PROPOSALS at the start of your application. If you don't it will be automatically rejected.

title: Sales Closer & DM Setter (Facebook Leads) | Fast-Paced Chat Support Role
description:

/Setters & Lead Managers to join our team.

mber will have Sunday off (PH time)

mber will have Monday off (PH time)

title: Illustrator & Animation skilled Storyteller
description:
Creative Illustrator & Motion Designer — Promo Punks



Full-Time | Remote | USD $1,350/month



Promo Punks is an Australian promotional products company that doesn't do boring. We're looking for a rare creative — someone who can draw, animate, edit, and make people care — to own our visual identity across social media and web.



This isn't a role where you follow templates. You'll concept, illustrate, animate, edit, and ship scroll-stopping content that drives real business outcomes. If your portfolio makes people pause mid-scroll, keep reading.



What you'll do:



- Create illustrated and animated content for social media ads (Meta, TikTok, YouTube), organic posts, and website assets

- Edit and produce short-form video content optimised for each platform

- Develop visual narratives that turn promotional products into something people actually want to engage with

- Take a brief — or sometimes just a loose idea — and turn it into a compelling piece from storyboard through to final delivery

- Maintain and evolve a consistent brand aesthetic while pushing creative boundaries



Skills & experience required:



- Outstanding illustration ability — character work, product illustration, editorial style, or a signature look that sets you apart

- Strong motion design and animation skills (After Effects, Animate, Rive, or equivalent)

- Solid video editing chops — you can cut, pace, and polish short-form content that performs (Premiere Pro, DaVinci Resolve, CapCut, or equivalent)

- A natural instinct for storytelling — you understand pacing, hooks, and emotional beats, not just pretty frames

- Proven experience creating content for social media and paid advertising

- Self-directed — you manage your own time and hit deadlines without hand-holding

- A portfolio that genuinely impresses



Nice to have:



- Experience with e-commerce or product-based brands

- Familiarity with the Australian market

- Experience with AI-assisted creative workflows



Compensation:



USD $1,350 per month, full-time. We're based in Australia (Brisbane, AEST) so some timezone overlap is preferred but not mandatory.



To apply:



Send your portfolio and a short note explaining which piece in it best shows your ability to combine illustration, animation, and storytelling — and why. 

Also, send a 10 Sec animation showing one of our products and how you imagine advertising it.

title: Senior QA/QC Architect
description:
******TO BE CONSIDERED FOR THIS ROLE, YOU MUST SUBMIT YOUR RESUME AND VIDEO INTRODUCTION.******



Senior QA/QC Architect 



Job Type: Full-Time

Work Hours: Monday to Friday, 9:00 AM to 5:30 PM Pacific Time, with a 30-minute break

Salary: $1,200 per month



Job Overview: 

We are seeking a Senior QA/QC Architect based in the Philippines to provide quality assurance and quality control oversight for architectural drawings produced by our drafting team. This role will focus on reviewing drawings, identifying coordination issues, and ensuring that all documentation meets technical and constructability standards before project delivery.

The ideal candidate is a licensed architect with strong experience working in reputable architectural firms on complex projects, with deep expertise in technical detailing, multidisciplinary coordination, and drawing review.



* Key Responsibilities:

Perform QA/QC reviews of architectural drawings produced by the drafting and production teams.

Identify nonconformance issues, coordination conflicts, and detailing errors within project drawings.

Review coordination between Architectural, Structural, and MEP consultant drawings.

Ensure drawings meet constructability requirements and professional architectural standards.

Provide clear feedback and correction notes to the drafting team to improve drawing accuracy and consistency.

Help maintain quality standards across projects and reduce repetitive corrections.

Ensure lessons learned from previous projects are applied to future projects to improve overall drawing quality.

Review and validate technical details and construction documentation before final submission.

Support internal teams by ensuring drawings follow company standards and project documentation requirements.



* Training & Standards

The selected candidate will be trained on the company’s storage system detailing standards and internal documentation processes to ensure alignment with project requirements.



* Qualifications:

Licensed Architect (Philippines) required

Minimum 6+ years of architectural experience in a reputable architectural firm

Experience working on complex architectural projects

Strong expertise in architectural detailing and construction documentation

Strong understanding of coordination between Architectural, Structural, and MEP disciplines

Ability to identify constructability and coordination issues within drawings



* Technical Skills:

Strong experience reviewing drawings in Revit

Working knowledge of AutoCAD

Ability to review and coordinate multi-disciplinary drawing sets



* Additional Requirements

Candidates must be able to provide examples of previous projects they have worked on

Strong attention to detail and problem-solving ability

Excellent ability to review and critique drawing packages for technical accuracy



Note: An NBI or police clearance will be required upon acceptance of the job offer.

s

2nd test)

title: Amazon Virtual Assistant
description:
Amazon PPC Assistant – Remote (Full-Time)



Are you detail-oriented, love working with data, and want to grow inside one of Europe's leading Amazon PPC agencies?

Then keep reading – this might be exactly the role you've been looking for.



About Us

We are Fox Performance, an Amazon PPC agency founded in 2018. We work mainly with German clients, but also serve international brands across Europe and the USA. Our clients come from all kinds of industries – beauty, supplements, home & living, electronics, pet products, fashion, you name it.



We're a team of true Amazon nerds: always on top of new features, always testing, always looking for the next edge to push our clients ahead. We don't believe in autopilot – we believe in craftsmanship, data, and constant improvement.

Now we're looking for an Amazon PPC Assistant to support our Amazon Account Managers and help us scale even faster.



What You'll Be Doing



Keyword Research – using tools like Helium 10, Cerebro, Brand Analytics and Amazon's own data to find the keywords that drive sales.

Campaign Setup – building out Sponsored Products, Sponsored Brands and Sponsored Display campaigns based on the structures and templates we've developed.

Bulk Sheet Work – preparing, editing and uploading Amazon bulk files cleanly and accurately.

Negative Keyword Mining – pulling search term reports, identifying waste, and adding negatives to keep ACoS healthy.

Reporting Support – helping prepare weekly and monthly client reports, pulling data from Seller Central, Helium 10 and our internal dashboards.

Listing Support – helping with keyword integration into titles, bullets and backend search terms.

Operational Tasks – supporting your Account Manager with whatever needs to be done to keep accounts running smoothly.



You will be the right hand of our PPC Managers – the person who keeps everything organized, accurate and on time.



What We're Looking For



At least 1 year of hands-on experience with Amazon Seller Central and/or Amazon Advertising Console or eCommerce work would be great BUT the most important thing for us is that you are completely reliable and are truly committed to growing your skills in the Amazon business, and that you’re willing to give it your all



Familiarity with Helium 10 (Cerebro, Magnet, Frankenstein) or similar tools.



Strong Excel / Google Sheets skills (filters, pivot tables, VLOOKUP / XLOOKUP – the basics that matter).



Excellent written and spoken English.



High attention to detail – you double-check your work before hitting submit.



Reliable, organized, and great at following SOPs.



A stable internet connection and a quiet remote work setup.



Bonus: experience with bulk file operations, Amazon DSP, or German marketplace (DE).





WHAT WE DON'T NEED

Whether you're looking for a temporary position, have multiple jobs at once, or just want to take a break from life—we're looking for top-notch part-time workers



What We Offer

The Essentials



100% Remote – work from anywhere in the Philippines.



Full-time, long-term position – we're looking for someone to grow with us, not a short-term gig.



Competitive salary in USD, paid reliably every month. (no limits on the long run)





If you read the full Text start your message with "HI WOLF"

What You'll Do:

- Strong eye for what makes content engaging — hooks, pacing, text placement

- Ability to turn around edits quickly — we move fast and test a lot

Bonus Points:

- Understanding of direct response advertising (hooks that stop the scroll, CTAs that convert)

- Experience with thumbnail creation

- Spanish language skills (we serve Spanish-speaking audiences)



Why Join Us:



- Fully remote — work from anywhere

- Creative work with real impact — your ads directly drive revenue

- Small, fast-moving team — lots of ownership, no bureaucracy

- Consistent workload with clear deliverables



To Apply:

title: Creative Strategist
description:
We’re looking for a creative, research-driven Creative Strategist who wants to grow long-term with our team as we scale digital ecommerce and SaaS businesses. You will spend 100% of your time researching new creatives for Meta ads, writing ad scripts, communicating with UGC creators and video editors, and finding inspiration from competitors and people in other industries.



We’ve been building in SaaS and ecommerce for 5+ years, and we are now doubling down on digital products, starting with 
 and several additional projects currently in development.



If you love creative strategy, testing new ad angles aggressively, and pushing the boundaries of direct response marketing, this role is for you.



ABOUT THIS ROLE:

You will be responsible for researching, ideating, and producing high-performing ad creatives, primarily for Meta ads. Our goal is to drive profitable front-end acquisition consistently at scale (2+ ROAS) through innovative, high-converting creative concepts.



You will not just write scripts, you will also lead creative experimentation, working with UGC creators, video editors, and AI UGC tools to produce breakthrough direct response ads. You’re also welcome to edit videos yourself when you have the time and interest. 



THIS ROLE IS FOR SOMEONE WHO:

Lives and breathes creative strategy, loves researching ad trends and competitor campaigns, and wants to test new hooks, scripts, and angles aggressively. You should be comfortable communicating with creators and editors, and want to grow long-term with a team that takes initiative and constantly looks for new creative opportunities.



RESPONSIBILITIES:

Your main focus will be researching new creatives for Meta ads daily, writing compelling ad scripts and hooks, and coordinating with UGC creators and video editors to bring those ideas to life. A big part of the job is finding inspiration on x.com, studying competitors, and looking at people in completely different industries for fresh angles.



You’ll constantly try new ways of creating ads, including AI UGC, and you’ll lead creative strategy and production end-to-end. If you have time and interest, you can also jump into video editing yourself.



REQUIREMENTS:

You should have real experience with creative strategy for direct response ads, writing ad scripts and hooks that convert, and managing UGC creators and content workflows. A strong understanding of Meta Ads creative best practices, plus a habit of researching trends and competitor ads, is essential. You’ll also need to communicate clearly with video editors and creators, and love running creative experiments.



YOU SHOULD ALSO:

Be open-minded and eager to try new creative approaches, active on x.com and following ad industry trends, highly proactive and independent, with strong creative thinking and writing skills.



HIGHLY PREFERRED EXPERIENCE:

Experience working with or producing creative for quiz-funnel based subscription brands, such as BetterMe, Coursiv, Hint, Astroline, Nebula, Simple Life App, Reverse Health, MadMuscles, Liven, or similar quiz-funnel driven digital subscription brands.



Big plus if you also know a skilled video editor who could work full or part time for us.



COMPENSATION:

Starting salary is $800/month based on experience and track record, with performance-based bonuses and long-term growth opportunities. Our goal is to build a small but extremely strong team that can scale multiple subscription businesses together over the next several years.



HOW TO APPLY:

When applying, please include:

- Your resume

- Your experience with creative strategy and ad production

- Examples of ad scripts or hooks you've written

- Types of Meta ad campaigns you've worked on

- Results you've achieved (CTR, conversion rates, etc.)

- Your experience with UGC creators and video editors

- Examples of creative angles or campaigns you’ve developed



Applications without specific creative examples may not be reviewed. If you're a serious Creative Strategist who wants to push boundaries in direct response marketing, we'd love to hear from you.

title: Telemarketer for lead generating
description:
Job Title: Remote Telemarketer for booking meetings (for Technical Product)



We are seeking an experienced and motivated Remote Telemarketer to perform outbound calling, identify opportunities, and booking qualified meetings for our field sales team. This is a part-time role (UK hours only 8:00 AM–12:00 PM) with a mandatory 8:00 AM daily huddle to be joined by teams. The position offers the potential to transition into full-time based on performance. The ideal candidate must be highly experienced in telemarketing and able to communicate technical concepts with clear spoken english.



Key Responsibilities

- Making high volume of outbound calls to targeted prospective customers using our VOIP 3CX phone system 

- Clearly introduce and explain a technical product to potential clients.

- typical 15-20 calls/hour

- Qualify prospects and schedule meetings for the sales team.

- Attend the daily 8:00 AM huddle.

- Maintain accurate call logs, lead information, and follow-up notes in our CRM.

- Follow up with warm leads and re-engage older prospects.

-Consistently meet or exceed weekly/monthly KPIs for calls, leads, and booked meetings.



Requirements

- Minimum 3 years of telemarketing experience — this is essential.

- Strong English speaking skills with a clear, confident, professional phone presence.

- Science or technical background preferred, ideally degree qualified (chemistry, biology, engineering, materials science, etc.).

-Proven ability to handle outbound calling, overcome objections, and secure appointments.

- Ability to quickly understand technical product details and communicate them simply.

- Reliable internet connection and quiet remote workspace.

- Comfortable using VOIP systems (training provided for 3CX).

- Strong interpersonal and organizational skills.

- Availability to work 8:00 AM–12:00 PM, Monday to Friday.

- Willingness to grow into a full-time position based on performance and meetings booked

- familiar with microsoft products such as outlook calendar, for sheduling meetings



Compensation

 bonus based on meetings booked and attended



Nice to Have

- Experience booking meeting for similar technical product, to FMCG markets - such as food factories

- Familiarity with CRM systems such as superoffice, HubSpot or Salesforce.

- Prior experience booking B2B sales meetings.



What We Offer

- Part-time (UK) hours (8 AM–12 PM) with potential for full-time promotion.

- USD4.40/hour + incentives.

- Training on our technical product and 3CX VOIP system.

- Supportive team and structured processes with scripts to follow



How to Apply:

Please send you CV in along with a short 2-3 minute video clip with you explaining why you believe you are a good fit for this role.

title: Short-Form Video Editor
description:
We're looking for a reliable video editor to help produce short-form content daily. The work is straightforward with clear daily direction provided.



**What you'll do:**

- Receive daily direction with a reference video and instructions

- Source raw clips from TikTok based on the reference provided

- Edit clips into 7–11 second videos in CapCut

- Deliver all clips to a shared Google Drive folder by 8am US Central Time



**What we're looking for:**

- CapCut experience — cuts, speed adjustments, cropping, zoom, audio layering

- Able to search and source clips on TikTok by topic or style

- Consistent and reliable daily output

- Follows reference examples closely and takes direction well

- Quick to respond when messaged

- Prior experience editing or sourcing content for affiliate marketing is a plus



**Schedule:**

- Monday through Saturday, Sunday off

- Philippine public holidays observed

- Flexible working hours — work whenever suits you as long as clips are in Google Drive by 8am US Central Time daily

- This is a full-time role

All work, content, and communications related to this role are strictly confidential. This is non-negotiable.



**Pay:**

- Base: $500/month

- Pay will depend on output quantity and quality over time

**To apply:**

Begin your application with the word "Orange." Send a short intro, confirm CapCut experience, state your preferred working hours, mention any affiliate marketing or related content experience, and confirm you have read the full post.

title: CAD DRAFTER
description:
Join our fast-growing architectural firm located in the vibrant city of Cupertino CA, backed by AlphaX RE Capital. With an array of services covering the entire real estate spectrum, from land acquisition to property listing, our firm offers a unique environment for growth and learning.

This role requires strong attention to detail, technical knowledge, and the ability to collaborate with engineers, architects, and project managers to bring designs to life.

Develop and revise detailed technical drawings, blueprints, and schematics using CAD software.

Qualifications

Associate’s or Bachelor’s degree in Drafting, Engineering Technology, Architecture, or related field.

Proficiency in CAD software (AutoCAD, Revit, SolidWorks, or similar).

Strong knowledge of drafting standards, construction methods, and technical terminology.

Ability to read and interpret engineering and architectural drawings.

Excellent attention to detail and organizational skills.

Strong communication and teamwork abilities.

Experience in [industry-specific focus, e.g., mechanical, civil, electrical, or architectural drafting] preferred.





Preferred Skills

Familiarity with Building Information Modeling (BIM).

Experience with 3D visualization tools.

Knowledge of project management software.

Ability to work under tight deadlines and manage multiple projects simultaneously.





Fully Remote

Schedule: 9:00 AM–5:30 PM PST, Monday–Friday (12:00 AM–8:30 AM PH Time)

title: Senior SDR - Mortgage & Lending
description:
About Zeitro

Zeitro helps mortgage professionals eliminate the daily grind of searching guidelines. Our Scenario AI delivers verified, lender-sourced answers in seconds — reducing guesswork, accelerating decisions, and increasing pull-through across wholesale, correspondent, retail, and broker channels.



The Role

You’ll own outbound growth across the mortgage ecosystem — engaging wholesale lenders, correspondent partners, broker-owners, branch managers, and top-producing loan officers nationwide. You understand the real pain points: overlays, AE delays, guideline ambiguity, lost deals, and slow turn times — and you know how to speak the language of each audience.

This role sits at the front line of revenue, partnering closely with Marketing, Product, and AEs to drive qualified demos, signups, and pipeline.



What You’ll Do

- Prospect and engage wholesale lenders, correspondent partners, brokers, and loan officers, targeting decision-makers from producing LOs to C-level, compliance, and product leaders.

- Execute full multi-channel outbound motions: cold calls, LinkedIn outreach, and personalized
 sequences.

- Research lender guideline structures and loan officer workflows to position Zeitro’s Scenario AI effectively.

- Educate prospects on the value of your product, qualify their interest, and convert conversations into demos or sign-ups.

- Partner with Marketing on localized campaigns, referral programs, and messaging refinements.

- Capture and relay market feedback from high-volume users to inform Product and GTM strategy.

- Maintain clean and accurate CRM data, ensuring strong handoffs to the AE team.





Requirements

- 3–5+ years of proven SDR/BDR or inside sales success in mortgage tech, FinTech, LOS, PPE, pricing engines related SaaS.

- Deep understanding of wholesale, correspondent, retail, and broker channel operations.

- Strong grasp of AE pipelines, underwriting guidelines, overlays, and pricing dynamics.

- Consistent track record of meetings or exceeding demo, meeting, or revenue targets.

- Confident communicator who builds trust quickly with LOs, brokers, branch managers, and executives.

- Tech-savvy with CRM and outbound tools (HubSpot, Salesforce, Apollo, Clay, sequencing platforms).

- Analytical, organized, and comfortable operating in a fast-moving startup environment.



Nice to Have

- Experience selling AI-driven or decision-support tools.

- Familiarity with compliance, guideline interpretation, or underwriting workflows.

- Experience supporting multi-stakeholder deals in complex sales cycles.

title: Accounts Payable
description:
ACCOUNTS PAYABLE JOB DESCRIPTION

We are looking for Accounts Payable Agents (AP CLERK) who will be based in their own home office.

This role is a full-time job, from Monday to Friday. 

Our aim is to form long- term relationships with our customers, suppliers, and staff to ensure that we build trust and ensure a sustainable high-quality of work end-to-end.



SBS is built around five key pillars:

1. Reducing our client’s expense bases

2. Sourcing sustainable products & services which lead to long term relationships.

3. Never compromising on quality

4. Future proofing companies processes by implementing controls & processes which are scalable up and down.

5. Respect, Respond and React to all our customer needs.



SKILLS AP CLERK

6.
 Management experience.

MUST HAVEs.

• Right attitude and work ethic in dealing with high-stake tasks and with little to no margin for errors.

• Preference is given to applicants who have SAP, XERO, or FILEBOUND experience.





REMUNERATION and WHAT WE PROVIDE

EQUIPMENT YOU NEED
