title: Product Manager — Auto Insurance Claim Tech Platform
description:
About Us:

Claim Factory is a technology-driven auto damage appraisal firm based in Denver, Colorado. We're building a systematized, AI-powered claims processing platform that outperforms traditional competitors on speed, accuracy, and consistency. AI is at the core of what we do — from automated QC scoring to narrative generation — and we're looking for someone to help us build it the right way.

The Role:

We need an AI-savvy Product Manager who can help us design smarter workflows, ensure our AI models are trained properly, and translate our operational processes into intelligent automation. You'll work directly with the founder to identify where AI can reduce friction, improve output quality, and compound value over time. Contract role starting at ten to fifteen hours per week with room to grow.

What You'll Do:

Identify opportunities to integrate or improve AI across our claims processing workflow. Help design and maintain prompt libraries, QC rule sets, and training datasets. Work with our team to ensure AI outputs are accurate, consistent, and carrier-compliant. Document AI model behavior, edge cases, and improvement opportunities. Translate founder vision and field feedback into actionable product requirements. Research and evaluate AI tools, APIs, and automation platforms relevant to our workflow.

What We're Looking For:

Hands-on experience working with AI tools — LLMs, prompt engineering, workflow automation, or model training. B2B product management or workflow design experience. Strong systems thinker who can spot inefficiencies and design smarter processes. Comfortable pushing back and asking hard questions — we want critical thinkers, not order takers. Strong written English and clear communicator.

Nice to Have:

Experience in insurance, claims, fintech, or automotive.(A Plus) Familiarity with Airtable, or similar platforms. Background at an early-stage startup.

Compensation:

Hourly contract, competitive rate based on experience.







PLEASE COMPLETE ASSESSMENT TO APPLY, APPLICATIONS WITHOUT ASSESSMENT ARE AUTO DENIED





Screening Questions — AI Product Manager, Claim Factory

Vision & Strategic Thinking



Describe a product you helped build or improve from the ground up. What was your role, what problem did it solve, and what made it stand out from competitors?

If you were building an AI-powered claims processing platform with the goal of selling the business in 3–5 years, what would you prioritize in year one and why?

What does a "best in class" B2B workflow product look like to you? Give us a real example of a product you admire and explain what makes it exceptional.



AI & Technical Depth



Walk us through a project where you worked directly with AI — whether prompt engineering, model training, workflow automation, or LLM integration. What was your specific contribution and what did you learn?

How would you approach building a QC scoring system that gets smarter over time — meaning it learns from errors and never repeats the same mistake twice? Walk us through your thinking.

What AI tools, APIs, or platforms are you currently working with or following closely? What excites you about where AI is heading in the next 12–24 months?



Product Execution



How do you prioritize a product roadmap when everything feels urgent? Walk us through your framework.

Describe a time you pushed back on a founder or senior leader's idea because you thought it was wrong. What happened and what was the outcome?

How do you validate that a feature is actually solving a customer problem before you build it?



Culture & Fit



We're a lean, remote team building a systematized business designed to run without any single person being a bottleneck. How do you work in that kind of environment and what do you need to thrive?

We're building Claim Factory to sell. That means every process, every system, and every product decision needs to be documented and transferable. How do you approach documentation and knowledge transfer in your work?

What would make you excited to work on this specific product — not just any product — and why?

title: IT Security & Cloud Engineer
description:
We are looking for an experienced IT Security & Cloud Engineer to join the team. This is a hybrid role combining hands-on service desk support with deep Microsoft Azure security and cloud engineering work. You will be the technical backbone of our managed service delivery — owning the day-to-day security posture of our financial services clients while contributing to broader security projects.



This is not a traditional helpdesk role. While you will handle escalated support, the majority of your time will be spent on meaningful security engineering work: deploying and tuning Defender for Endpoint, managing Entra ID Conditional Access, implementing Microsoft Purview DLP policies, and contributing to Essential Eight compliance programs.



Service Desk & Client Support

• Provide Level 1–3 IT support to clients across Microsoft 365 and Azure environments

• Manage end-to-end user onboarding and offboarding across M365, Entra ID, Intune, and related platforms

• Troubleshoot Windows 11, macOS, iOS, and Android devices; resolve Office 365 and Teams issues

• Handle escalated tickets from clients with professionalism and clear communication

• Maintain accurate documentation, SOPs, and knowledge base articles in ITGlue or equivalent

• Liaise with third-party vendors and Microsoft support when required



Microsoft Azure & Cloud Engineering

• Design, deploy, and manage Azure environments including Entra ID, Conditional Access, and Azure Policy

• Administer Azure Virtual Machines, Azure Backup, Azure Key Vault, and Azure Landing Zone configurations

• Manage Microsoft Intune for device compliance, MDM/MAM configuration profiles, and Autopatch

• Maintain Microsoft 365 licensing via Pax8 or CSP, including provisioning and optimisation

• Perform PowerShell scripting to automate routine administrative tasks across M365 and Azure

• Support Microsoft Copilot and Azure OpenAI deployments within secure, compliant environments



Cybersecurity & Security Operations

• Manage and tune Microsoft Defender for Endpoint, Defender for Office 365, and Defender for Cloud

• Deploy and maintain Microsoft Sentinel SIEM — create and manage Analytic Rules, Workbooks, and Playbooks (SOAR)

• Configure Microsoft Purview for Data Loss Prevention (DLP), Sensitivity Labels, and AI Hub monitoring

• Manage endpoint security platforms including SentinelOne, CrowdStrike, or equivalent EDR solutions

• Administer Proofpoint or similar
 security platforms for anti-phishing and threat protection

• Support phishing simulation campaigns and cyber awareness training delivery via KnowBe4 or Proofpoint

• Monitor security alerts, triage incidents, and participate in incident response activities

• Conduct log review and security scan analysis; escalate material incidents in line with APRA CPS 234 requirements

• Assist with Essential Eight (E8) maturity assessments, gap analysis, and remediation projects

• Support Privileged Identity Management (PIM) and Just-in-Time (JIT) access configuration



About You — The Ideal Candidate

We are looking for someone who thinks like a security professional first, and a support engineer second. You are passionate about the Microsoft security stack, proactive about staying current with the threat landscape, and comfortable communicating technical risk to non-technical stakeholders.



Essential Requirements

• Minimum 3 years of experience in an IT engineering or security role

• Demonstrated hands-on experience with Microsoft 365 administration at an expert level (Entra ID, Intune, Defender, Exchange, Purview, SharePoint, Teams)

• Strong working knowledge of Azure security services: Conditional Access, Azure AD / Entra ID P2, Azure Policy, and Key Vault

• Experience with EDR/XDR platforms — Microsoft Defender for Endpoint, SentinelOne, or CrowdStrike

• Familiarity with Microsoft Sentinel or equivalent SIEM platforms

• Solid understanding of cybersecurity fundamentals: identity, endpoint,
, and data security

• Strong written and verbal communication skills; able to explain technical concepts clearly to business stakeholders

• Self-motivated, detail-oriented, and comfortable working in a fast-paced boutique environment



Highly Desirable

• Experience with the Australian Cyber Security Centre (ACSC) Essential Eight framework

• Knowledge of APRA CPS 234, ASIC cybersecurity guidelines, or ISO 27001

• Experience with Microsoft Purview Compliance — DLP, Sensitivity Labels, Insider Risk Management

• Familiarity with KnowBe4 or Proofpoint Security Awareness platforms

• PowerShell scripting capability for M365 and Azure automation

• Prior experience in a managed service provider (MSP) or consulting environment

• Understanding of financial services compliance requirements in the Australian market





Desirable Certificates:

AZ-500: Azure Security Engineer

SC-200: Security Operations Analyst

MS-102: M365 Administrator Expert

SC-300: Identity & Access Admin

title: Meta Ads Manager
description:
Meta Ads Manager (Performance-Focused | Service-Based Business)



We’re looking for a Meta Ads Manager to support and scale our paid advertising efforts for a growing photography and service-based business.



This role is focused on performance, not vanity metrics. We care about bookings and revenue,  not just leads.



What You’ll Be Doing

Build and manage Meta ad campaigns 
 & Instagram)

Monitor performance and optimise campaigns daily/weekly

Scale winning creatives and kill underperforming ones

Maintain clean campaign structure and reporting

Work closely with our creative producer (who supplies ad creatives)

Follow a clear testing structure (angles, hooks, variations)

What You Won’t Be Doing

You won’t be responsible for creative production

You won’t need to design funnels or landing pages

You won’t be guessing strategy — direction will be provided

What We’re Looking For

2+ years experience running Meta ads

Strong understanding of campaign structure and optimisation

Experience working with creatives and testing variations

Ability to interpret data and make clear optimisation decisions

Comfortable working with service-based businesses (or willing to learn)

Bonus (Not Required)

Experience with offline conversions or CRM-based tracking

Understanding of conversion-focused landing pages

Familiarity with tools like Zapier, GHL, or similar

How We Work

We focus on creative-first testing (not over-complicated targeting)

We prioritise bookings and revenue over lead volume

We keep systems simple and scalable

To Apply



Please answer the following in as much technical detail as possible (avoid generic answers):



If a campaign is generating low-cost leads but very few bookings, how would you diagnose the issue step-by-step? Include what data you’d check and how you’d interpret it.

How do you determine when to scale a campaign? Include specific metrics, thresholds, and any changes you would make (budget, creatives, structure).

What metrics do you prioritise when managing campaigns, and how do you connect those metrics to actual revenue or conversions?

Describe your current campaign structure for Meta (campaign ? ad set ? ad level). How do you approach targeting in today’s Meta environment?



Include examples of campaigns you’ve worked on if possible.



We’re looking for someone reliable, performance-driven, and easy to work with, not someone chasing trends or vanity metrics.

title: Full-Time Designer
description:
About Premium Invention

Premium Invention is a leader in high-end solar security cameras, pushing the boundaries of innovation to deliver customer-centric solutions. We’re on a mission to scale our business to new heights, and we need bold, creative talent to help us get there. Join us to redefine security technology through exceptional design.



The Role

We’re searching for a passionate and competitive Designer to join our dynamic, remote team. You’ll collaborate with marketing, product, and engineering teams to create visually stunning, user-focused designs that drive our brand forward. Your work will blend aesthetics with psychological insight to captivate customers and elevate our products. If you’re ready to make an impact in a fast-paced, innovative environment, this is your chance.



Key Responsibilities

- Design high-quality graphics for marketing materials, branding, and product interfaces.

- Craft intuitive UX/UI for websites and applications using Figma.

- Work closely with cross-functional teams to align designs with business objectives.

- Apply psychological principles to create meaningful, customer-driven designs.

- Source compelling online content or leverage AI tools to enhance your work.

- (Optional) Deliver product renderings, packaging designs, or other creative assets.



Requirements

- Proven experience in graphic design and UX/UI, with advanced proficiency in Figma.

- Strong English proficiency for clear communication and collaboration.

- A portfolio showcasing your design expertise and customer-focused approach (required).

- Deep understanding of how psychology influences effective design.

- Ability to thrive in a collaborative, remote team environment.

- Bonus: Skills in product rendering, packaging design, or 3D tools (e.g., Blender).

- Bonus: Interest in solar technology or tech innovation.



Why This Role Matters

Your designs will define how customers experience our cutting-edge solar security solutions. From sleek interfaces to eye-catching marketing, you’ll shape the way the world sees Premium Invention—making security not just functional, but unforgettable.



What We Offer

- Competitive salary with monthly performance bonuses.

- A collaborative, innovative team environment (working with a 5-person marketing crew and engineers).

- The chance to grow with a rapidly scaling company—lead future projects and expand your career.

- A remote, fast-paced, startup-like culture that values creativity and results.



To Apply

Submit your resume, portfolio link, and answers to the screening questions below to [insert application
/link]. We’re excited to meet our next design rockstar!



Mandatory Screening Questions

- How many years have you actively worked in graphic design and UX/UI? Please highlight your experience with Figma.

- Walk us through your design process. How do you incorporate customer psychology or feedback to make your work effective?

- Describe a time you collaborated with a team on a design project. What was your role, and how did you handle feedback?

- How soon are you ready to start? We’re looking for someone to begin immediately.

- What’s one design project you’re proud of, and why? (Include a link if possible.)

title: Consumer Psychology & Conversion Strategist
description:
About Us:

We specialize in high-end, solar-powered security technology. Our mission is to deliver innovative, reliable products to residential, commercial, and remote property owners across the globe. As we expand, we're looking for a strategic thinker with a deep understanding of consumer behavior to help us refine our product positioning, improve conversion rates, and successfully launch into new international markets.



Role Overview:

We're seeking a Consumer Psychology & Conversion Strategist who understands the science of selling. Your mission is to plan, optimize, and execute product positioning strategies across our Shopify store, Amazon listings, and new geo-market expansions. You must be highly skilled in applying psychological principles to digital sales, crafting persuasive messaging, and adapting to different cultural audiences.



You will work closely with our CEO, marketing team, designers, and developers to continuously improve the customer journey and maximize conversion rates.





Key Responsibilities:



- Product Page Optimization:

- Design and structure high-converting Shopify product pages.

- Build persuasive content flows based on consumer behavior psychology.



Amazon Marketplace Strategy:

- Strategize and execute highly optimized Amazon product listings (title, bullets, A+ content, images).

- Plan customer journey from ad click to purchase with maximum trust triggers.



Consumer Research & Geo-Market Adaptation:

- Conduct cultural psychology research to adapt messaging for markets such as USA, Canada, Australia, UK, Germany, Netherlands.

- Recommend copy, visuals, offers, and CTAs that align with local buying behaviors.



Copywriting & Messaging Strategy:

- Write or oversee persuasive, psychologically driven copy for pages, ads, and
 flows.



Conversion Rate Optimization (CRO):

- A/B test headlines, page layouts, offers, and customer journeys.

- Analyze page heatmaps and visitor recordings to identify friction points.



Collaborative Leadership:

- Work cross-functionally with design, development, marketing, and marketplace teams.



Key Skills & Qualifications:

- 3+ years experience in e-commerce, CRO, or conversion-focused marketing.

- Deep understanding of consumer psychology and behavioral science.

- Strong copywriting skills with proven examples.

- Experience with Shopify, Amazon Seller Central, Klaviyo, and basic SEO.

- Strong analytical skills — comfortable interpreting data, running A/B tests, and proposing new strategies.

- Adaptability to different cultures and consumer mindsets.

- Clear, confident communication and project management skills.



Bonus Skills:

- Familiarity with tools like Hotjar, Lucky Orange, Microsoft Clarity (for behavioral analysis).

- Prior experience launching into international markets.

- UI/UX background or understanding of page flow best practices.





What We Offer:

- Competitive salary with performance bonuses

- Opportunity to work directly with the CEO and leadership team

- Full creative freedom to shape customer experience and brand voice

- Growth path to leadership roles as our brand expands globally

- A company that values psychology, precision, and winning the long game

title: Data Auditor — Claims Operations
description:
About Us:

Claim Factory is a technology-driven auto damage appraisal firm building a systematized, AI-powered claims platform. Accuracy and completeness of our production data is critical to our business — every claim, supplement, and file needs to be tracked, verified, and accounted for in our CRM.

The Role:

We need a detail-obsessed Data Auditor to review completed claim files and ensure everything is properly logged, categorized, and complete in our system. You'll be the last line of defense between our production workflow and our data integrity. This is a part-time hourly contract role with minimum billable hours to start.

What You'll Do:

Review completed claim files against CRM records to ensure accuracy and completeness. Verify that all supplements, photos, and supporting documents are properly attached and logged. Flag missing, incomplete, or inconsistent records for correction. Maintain an audit log of findings and resolutions. Work closely with the Product Manager and operations team to identify patterns and systemic gaps.

What We're Looking For:

Strong attention to detail — this role lives and dies by accuracy. Experience with CRM platforms, data entry, or operations administration. Comfortable working in Airtable or similar tools. Ability to follow structured checklists and SOPs precisely. Self-directed and reliable with minimal supervision.

Nice to Have:

Background in insurance, claims processing, or auto industry. Experience in data quality, compliance, or audit roles. Familiarity with document management workflows.

Compensation:

Hourly contract, competitive rate based on experience.





SCREENING QUESTIONS — Data Auditor

Attention to Detail & Process



Describe a role where data accuracy was critical to your work. What systems did you use and how did you ensure nothing slipped through?

Walk us through how you would audit a claim file to verify it is complete and properly logged in a CRM. What would you check and in what order?

Have you ever caught a significant error or pattern of errors in a dataset? What was it, how did you find it, and what did you do about it?



Tools & Systems



What CRM or database platforms have you worked with? How comfortable are you learning new tools quickly?

If you were given a checklist of fifty claim files to audit in Airtable and found that thirty percent had missing supplements, how would you document, escalate, and track resolution of those findings?

How do you stay organized when managing multiple audit tasks with different priorities and deadlines?



Work Style & Fit



This role requires working independently with minimal supervision. How do you structure your day and hold yourself accountable to deadlines?

Describe a time you identified a systemic problem — not just a one-off error — through your audit work. How did you communicate it and what changed as a result?

We are a lean, fast-moving team. Processes will evolve as we grow. How do you handle working in an environment where SOPs are still being developed?

title: WordPress / Elementor Developer for Construction & Home Services Marketing Agency

QA your own work across desktop, tablet, and mobile before handoff

Use AI tools (Claude, ChatGPT) to work faster without losing quality

title: HR & Roster Coordinator — Field Operations
description:
HR & Roster Coordinator — Field Operations

About Us:

Claim Factory is a technology-driven auto damage appraisal firm based in Denver, Colorado. We're building a national roster of independent field appraisers across multiple markets to support our direct carrier relationships. We need someone to help us build, manage, and maintain that roster so we're ready to deploy when clients come calling.

The Role:

We need an organized, proactive HR and Roster Coordinator to manage our independent appraiser pipeline. You'll handle everything from job postings and candidate outreach to license verification, background checks, and contracts. This is a part-time hourly contract role. You won't just be filling seats — you're helping us build the operational backbone of a national field network.

What You'll Do:

Post and manage job listings on LinkedIn and other relevant platforms to attract independent appraisers across target markets. Collect and verify appraiser credentials including licenses, addresses, and certifications. Coordinate background checks and ensure all compliance documentation is complete. Manage appraiser contracts and onboarding paperwork. Maintain an organized roster database with market coverage, availability, and status. Identify coverage gaps in key markets and proactively recruit to fill them.

What We're Looking For:

Experience in HR coordination, recruiting, or talent operations. Highly organized with strong follow-through — nothing falls through the cracks. Comfortable managing multiple candidates and documents simultaneously. Proficient with LinkedIn Recruiter or similar platforms. Strong written English and professional communication skills.

Nice to Have:

Experience in insurance, field services, or gig-economy workforce management. Background in contractor or vendor onboarding. Familiarity with compliance documentation and credentialing workflows.

Compensation:

Hourly contract, competitive rate based on experience.

title: Marketing manager
description:
About the Role

Protek Pest Control is looking for a visionary Chief Marketing Officer to lead our marketing strategy and drive growth in a competitive home-services market. As CMO, you will be responsible for building and executing a comprehensive marketing plan that positions Protek as the trusted, go-to pest control provider in our region. You will own our brand voice, customer acquisition channels, and long-term growth initiatives across digital, traditional, and community-based marketing.

This is a high-impact leadership role reporting directly to the owner. You will shape how homeowners and businesses discover, trust, and choose Protek for all their pest control needs.

What You’ll Do



Develop and lead the company’s overall marketing strategy, including digital, social, SEO, PPC,
, content, and traditional advertising

Build and manage a high-performing marketing team (or agency partnerships) focused on lead generation and brand awareness

Oversee all customer-facing messaging, website, and online presence to ensure a consistent, professional brand experience

Create targeted campaigns for residential and commercial pest control services (ants, rodents, termites, bed bugs, etc.)

Analyze performance data and optimize marketing efforts for maximum ROI and lead quality

Partner closely with sales and operations teams to align marketing with real customer needs and service delivery

 automation, and analytics tools)

title: Sales Manager / Appointment Setter
description:
Baird Built

New Zealand



About Us

We are growing and want someone who can help manage new enquiries, follow up leads properly, organise appointments, and create new opportunities through outbound calling and relationship building.

ntum in the sales process.



The Role

You will help manage incoming leads, follow up quotes and proposals, coordinate pre-visit packs, do outbound calling, and organise meetings for the Director with key referrers and potential partners.



This role is not just admin. We want someone who can take ownership, follow through, and help drive sales activity.



Main Responsibilities



Respond to new enquiries quickly

Make fast first contact with new leads so opportunities do not go cold

Qualify leads using our process

Follow up enquiries, quotes, and proposals consistently

Book appointments, calls, and site visits

Send client materials by
 where required

Coordinate physical lookbook sending by notifying NZ staff with correct client details

Confirm client name, address, phone number,
, project type, and timing before dispatch

Update CRM or tracking sheets when lookbooks are requested, sent, and received

Cold call potential clients and referral partners

Reconnect with older leads

Identify architects, designers, agents, developers, and other potential referral partners

Reach out to key referrers and potential partners to introduce Baird Built

Organise meetings, calls, or coffee catch-ups for the Director with qualified referrers and strategic partners

Keep a pipeline of referral and partnership opportunities moving

Help move leads from enquiry to signed job

Report on lead activity and pipeline progress



What We Need



Strong spoken and written English

Proven sales, appointment setting, lead generation, or business development experience

Strong follow-up habits

Confident on the phone

Able to respond quickly and stay on top of new enquiries

Good organisation and attention to detail

Able to work independently

Reliable internet and quiet work setup



Nice to Have



Experience in construction, real estate, property, or home services

CRM experience

Experience with outbound sales or cold calling

Experience coordinating admin workflows and handover tasks

Experience booking meetings with business owners or decision-makers



What We Are Looking For



Someone proactive

Someone fast to respond

Someone who follows through

Someone confident but not pushy

Someone who can build trust and keep leads moving

Someone who can manage details accurately and communicate clearly with our NZ team

Someone who can help open doors to valuable referral relationships



There is also room for performance bonuses based on results such as:



qualified appointments booked

referrer or partner meetings organised

strong enquiry follow-up

signed jobs from leads you have helped manage

title: Structural Engineer — Steel Building Design (Eurocode 3, UK/Ireland, Ongoing Work)
description:
Jet Steel Commercial Consultants Ltd is a structural steel consultancy based in Northern Ireland. We provide estimating, commercial management and QS services to structural steel contractors across the UK and Ireland. We are now launching a range of pre-designed hot rolled structural steel buildings — garages, workshops, field shelters and agricultural stores — for supply and install to domestic, equestrian and agricultural customers across Northern Ireland and the Republic of Ireland.

We already have an experienced Philippine detailer on our team. We are now looking for a qualified Structural Engineer to work alongside them on an ongoing project basis.



This is a long-term freelance position. We are not looking for a one-off job. We are building a product range that will generate repeat engineering work week after week as orders come in.

Phase 1 — Standard size packs (immediate)

We need structural calculations and connection designs produced for two pilot building sizes first, then the full range once quality is confirmed:

Pilot Building 1 — JS-G1 Garage/Workshop

 • Span: 6000mm

 • Length: 6000mm (2 bays @ 3000mm)

 • Eave height: 2700mm

 • Roof pitch: 10°

 • Location: County Fermanagh, Northern Ireland (Wind Zone 3, Snow Zone to UK NA)

 • Frame: Hot rolled UC columns + IPE/UB rafters, haunched eave connection

 • Finish: Hot dip galvanised throughout

 • Openings: 1 no. roller door 3000×2400mm, 1 no. personnel door 900×2100mm, 1 no. window 600×600mm

 • Foundation: Pad footings — base plate and holding down bolt design required

Pilot Building 2 — JS-A1 Agricultural Store

 • Span: 9000mm

 • Length: 12000mm (4 bays @ 3000mm)

 • Eave height: 4000mm

 • Roof pitch: 10°

 • Location: County Fermanagh, Northern Ireland

 • Frame: Hot rolled UC columns + UB rafters with haunch, wind posts to gable ends

 • Finish: Hot dip galvanised throughout

 • Openings: 1 no. roller door 4000×3600mm, 1 no. personnel door 900×2100mm

 • Foundation: Pad footings — base plate and holding down bolt design required

For each building you must produce:

 • Full structural calculations to Eurocode 3 (BS EN 1993-1-1) and BS EN 1991 loading

 • Wind loading to BS EN 1991-1-4 with UK National Annex — Fermanagh postcode (BT74)

 • Snow loading to BS EN 1991-1-3 with UK National Annex

 • Member design — columns, rafters, haunches, purlins, side rails, wind bracing

 • Connection design — eave, apex, base plate, holding down bolts to BS EN
• Foundation design or holding down bolt schedule for pad footings

 • Calculation pack in PDF format suitable for NI Building Control submission

 • Section schedule listing all steel sections required with weights

Phase 2 — Full standard range (following quality confirmation)

Once we are satisfied with your Phase 1 work, we will commission the full range — approximately 10 standard building sizes covering garages, workshops, field shelters and agricultural stores. These will become our standard product drawings, reused across multiple customer orders.

Ongoing work:

Every customer order that falls outside a standard size will require a bespoke calculation. We expect 2–5 of these per month once trading. This is your ongoing income stream.



WHAT WE NEED FROM YOU

 • Degree in Civil or Structural Engineering

 • Minimum 3 years experience in structural steel design

 • Proven competence in Eurocode 3 — you must provide sample calculations with your application

 • Experience with UK National Annex loading — wind and snow

 • Software: TEDDS, Tekla Structural Designer, or equivalent calculation software (not just hand calcs)

 • Good written English — your calculation packs go to UK Building Control

 • 48–72 hour turnaround on standard jobs

 • Available on WhatsApp or
 for queries

Nice to have:

 • Previous experience working with UK or Irish engineers or contractors

 • Familiarity with CE marking to EN 1090

 • Experience producing pad footing / foundation designs



WHAT WE OFFER

 • Phase 1 pilot (2 buildings): fixed fee — state your rate in application

 • Phase 2 standard range (approx. 8–10 further sizes): fixed fee per building

 • Ongoing bespoke orders: per-job rate agreed upfront

 • Long term relationship — we are building a product business, not a one-off project

 • Direct communication with the company director

 • Clear briefs, fast decisions, prompt payment

Approximate volume once trading: 2–4 bespoke jobs per month plus repeat use of standard size packs across multiple customer orders.





HOW TO APPLY

Send the following:

 1. Your CV including qualifications and relevant experience

 2. Two examples of previous Eurocode 3 structural steel calculations you have produced — these must show member design and connection design, not just loading

 3. Your proposed fixed fee for Phase 1 (both pilot buildings)

 4. Your proposed rate per building for Phase 2

 5. Your proposed rate per bespoke job for ongoing work

 6. Confirmation of your software and calculation tools

 7. Confirmation you are available within 48–72 hours for standard jobs

Applications without sample calculations will not be considered.

title: Senior Google Ads Specialist
description:
*Senior PPC Manager*



About Us

We are Matt & Markus - Two experienced Online Marketing Experts with a combined 20+ years of PPC experience. We specialize in running high performing Google Ads campaigns. Additionally, we support clients with in depth data analysis and conversion optimization. 



We have been working independently as freelancers for many years and teamed up to build and grow our Google Ads agency in 2025. The agency is growing and with that growth we are building a strong team to be part of the journey.



About the Role

We are a growing performance marketing agency focused on delivering data-driven PPC campaigns for international clients. Our main expertise lies within Google Ads.

We are looking for a Senior PPC Manager with several (4+) years of experience with Google Ads to take ownership of our PPC accounts, drive growth, and operate with a high level of independence.

You will work closely with the founders while also owning execution, strategy, and performance across accounts.



Key Responsibilities

- Account Ownership & Performance

- Manage PPC accounts end-to-end across Google Ads (Search, Performance Max, Display, YouTube)

- Take full ownership of results (scaling volume, improving performance, driving growth)

- Identify and act on opportunities without needing constant direction

- Scaling & Strategy

- Scale campaigns efficiently while maintaining performance targets

- Allocate budgets based on performance trends and growth opportunities

- Build and refine account structures to support long-term scaling

- Data, Tracking & Insights



Work confidently with:

- GA4 (setup, analysis, debugging)

- Google Tag Manager (tracking setup, event tracking)

- Offline Conversion Tracking (OCT)

- Looker Studio dashboards (build and interpret)

- Turn data into clear, actionable insights that drive decisions



Testing & Optimisation

- Run structured testing plans (ads, bidding strategies, landing pages)

- Continuously improve CVR, CTR, and overall account efficiency

- Identify performance issues early and resolve them proactively



Client Communication

- Communicate directly with clients in a clear and confident manner

- Present performance updates and explain strategy in simple terms

- Build trust through ownership and results



Mentorship

- Support and mentor junior tea
mbers when needed

- Share knowledge and best practices to improve team performance



What We’re Looking For



Experience

- 4+ years hands-on PPC experience (primarily Google Ads)

- Agency or client-side experience required

- Proven ability to scale campaigns and drive growth

- Experience managing multiple accounts independently



Technical Skills

- Strong expertise in Google Ads.

- Solid understanding of:

- GA4 + GTM

- Conversion tracking and attribution

- Offline conversions

- Looker Studio



Mindset

- Highly proactive and accountable

- Strong problem solver who figures things out independently

- Commercially aware (focus on business outcomes, not just metrics)

- Comfortable making decisions without constant guidance



Important (Read Carefully)



We value:

- Ownership (and being able to take responsibility for wins and mistakes)

- Critical thinking (and being able to say “I see it different”)

- Clear communication when something is unclear (and being able to say “I don’t know”)

- Someone who enjoys life and is generally positive and can laugh while delivering high quality work



We are not looking for someone who:

- Relies heavily on AI tools without understanding the work

- Says they understand but does not ask questions when unsure





Working Setup

- Remote (Philippines-based)

- Results are more important than a work schedule

- Small, high-performance team

- Focus on results, not micromanagement



Salary

Based on experience



What Success Looks Like

- Accounts are scaling in volume and revenue

- Performance opportunities are identified and acted on proactively

- Minimal hand-holding required



How to Apply



Please include:

- Your CV

- Written Summary of your PPC experience and where you see your personal strength

- Examples of accounts you’ve scaled (include results)

- Tools and platforms you’ve used

- Any other online marketing experience



We are looking forward to receive your application and to get to know you.



Best wishes,

Markus and Matt

title: Senior Design Associate (PH)

.

• Strong organizational skills, high attention to detail, and accuracy in documentation.

Fully Remote

title: HR COORDINATOR
description:
**HR & Recruitment Coordinator (People-First, High-Impact Role)**



We’re looking for a sharp, people-first **HR & Recruitment Coordinator** who is technically savvy, highly organized, and ready to take ownership from day one.



This is not a placeholder role — we’re building something real, and we need someone who wants to grow with it.



## **WHAT THIS ROLE OWNS**



**Recruitment (50%)**

* Support and execute recruitment processes — sourcing, screening, and coordinating interviews

* Work closely with leadership to identify and attract **high-quality candidates**

* Maintain an organized and efficient candidate pipeline

* Ensure a strong candidate experience from first touchpoint to onboarding



**Client & Growth Support (25%)**



* Assist in identifying and organizing potential client or candidate opportunities

* Support outreach coordination and follow-ups (non-sales focused)

* Help maintain internal tracking systems and pipelines

* Ensure smooth coordination between recruitment efforts and business needs



**Operations & HR Support (25%)**



* Support onboarding processes to ensure new hires are set up for success

* Assist with offboarding and transitions when needed

* Maintain HR documentation, contracts, and internal systems (Notion)

* Help track team compliance and internal processes

* Support day-to-day operational needs as the company grows



**YOU'RE THE RIGHT PERSON IF YOU:**



* Have solid HR or recruitment experience and understand people, process, and accountability

* Are strong at sourcing and identifying the **right candidates, not just filling roles**

* Are highly proficient in **Notion** and enjoy organizing systems

* Have experience with **AI tools (e.g., Claude, ChatGPT)** and are eager to use them to work smarter

* Are technically savvy and quick to learn new tools

* Communicate proactively — you update before being asked

* Can handle sensitive situations with professionalism and discretion

* Thrive in a fast-moving, high-standard environment

* Genuinely care about people and team culture



**THIS IS NOT FOR YOU IF YOU:**

* View HR as purely administrative

* Are looking for a short-term or side role



**TO APPLY**

Send your resume + a short 1–2 minute introduction.

We move fast — if you’re the right fit, you’ll know quickly.

title: Executive assistant / Operations manager
description:

**Type:** Full-time

**Reports to:** CEO (Apeing & Fuel Nutrition)



Apeing is a fast-moving Web3/DeFi project building next-gen crypto trading and creator experiences.

Fuel Nutrition is a high-growth DTC beauty & wellness brand scaling through social and affiliate-driven e-commerce.



Role Summary



We’re hiring a proactive, detail-obsessed operator to be the CEO’s right hand across two companies. You’ll own weekly execution: driving projects to done, unblocking teams, hiring great people, and keeping the CEO’s schedule, recruiting pipeline, and communications crisp. On the Fuel side you’ll run the e-commerce operating rhythm and manage the store team. On the Apeing side you’ll function as an Executive Assistant / Chief-of-Staff-lite to ensure velocity.



---



## Core Responsibilities



### 1) Fuel Nutrition — Operations Management



* **Weekly Operating Rhythm:** Own the Monday plan ? Friday wrap cadence; ensure >90% completion of weekly priorities.

* **Project Management:** Maintain the master roadmap (launches, promos, inventory, CX initiatives). Break projects into tasks, owners, deadlines; monitor burn-down and remove blockers.

* **Team Management:** Lead daily standups; assign work to CX, content, design, ops, and creators’ support; track performance and SLAs.

* **E-commerce Ops:** Oversee Shopify/Amazon/TikTok Shop readiness, PDP accuracy, pricing/promos, bundles, inventory ETAs, and merchandising calendar.

* **CX & Quality:** Ensure Gorgias/Helpdesk response times, returns/exchanges process quality, review sentiment, QA on shipments & packaging updates.

* **Vendors & Logistics:** Coordinate 3PL/manufacturing timelines, purchase orders, inbound/outbound shipments, and restock alerts.

* **Performance Tracking:** Build and maintain dashboards for GMV, AOV, CAC/ROAS (where applicable), fulfillment SLAs, creator GMV, and subscription metrics.

* **SOPs & Compliance:** Create/update SOPs for store ops, CX, creator payouts, coupon governance, and promo calendars.



2) Apeing Dex — Executive Assistant to the CEO



* **Inbox & Calendar Control:** Triage
/DMs, prep briefings, protect focus blocks, schedule internal/external meetings, assemble agendas & notes, and ensure follow-through.

* **Recruiting & Talent Pipeline:** Post roles on LinkedIn, screen applicants, run initial screens, manage the candidate tracker, schedule panels, and coordinate offers.

* **Project Coordination:** Track engineering/design/BD workstreams; document decisions; chase owners for updates; highlight risks and dependencies.

* **Documentation:** Prepare board/partner updates, meeting notes, lite research briefs (competitors, partners, ecosystems).





3) Cross-Company PMO (Both)



* **Single Source of Truth:** Maintain shared Notion/Trello/Asana boards with goals, owners, and timelines for both companies.

* **Weekly CEO Brief:** 1-pager with status, blockers, next actions, hiring updates, and decisions needed.

* **Bottleneck Radar:** Detect slippage early; propose options; escalate with data.

* **Confidential Initiatives:** Handle sensitive docs, contracts, and negotiations support with discretion.



---



## KPIs / Scorecard



* **Weekly task completion rate:** ? 90?ross active projects

* **Hiring pipeline:** Time-to-first-interview ? 5 business days; time-to-offer ? 25 days

* **Meeting hygiene:** 100% agendas sent ? 12 hrs prior; 100% notes + action items within 6 hrs

* **E-com ops SLAs:** 3PL on-time fulfillment ? 98%; CX first-response time ? 2 hrs (business hrs)

* **Data discipline:** Dashboards updated daily; zero stale metrics >24 hrs on critical boards



## Qualifications



* 4–7+ years in **operations/project management** and/or **EA/Chief-of-Staff** roles in fast-growing startups.

* Proven experience running an **e-commerce** operating cadence (Shopify/Amazon/TikTok Shop) **or** product/engineering coordination AND working in a tech or blockchain company a HUGE PLUS!

* Strong people management: running standups, assigning work, and holding owners accountable.

* Excellent written communication; executive-level judgment and confidentiality.

* Comfortable in **Web3/crypto** culture (you don’t need to be an engineer, but you speak the language and can track technical projects and talk to devs).

* Advanced proficiency with: Notion or Asana/Trello, Google Workspace, Slack/
, GSheets (lookups/pivots), Zoom/Calendly. Bonus: Shopify, Amazon Seller Central, Gorgias/Helpdesk, Phantom/Wallet basics, Figma.



---



## Tools You’ll Use (typical stack)



* **PM/Docs:** Notion, Asana/Trello, Google Docs/Sheets/Slides

* **Comms:** Slack, 
, Gmail, Zoom, Calendly

* **E-com:** Shopify, Amazon Seller Central, TikTok Shop, Gorgias/Helpdesk, 3PL portal

* **Analytics:** Looker/Datastudio or similar dashboards, ad platform dashboards

* **Web3 (nice-to-have):** Phantom, Solana explorers, Dune/Flipside (basic queries)



---



## Working Style



* Owner mindset, bias to action, and calm under pressure.

* Ruthless prioritization; you protect the CEO’s time and the teams’ throughput.

* You love clarity—clear goals, clear owners, clear due dates—and you enforce it.



---



## 30/60/90 Day Plan



**Day 30:** Implement weekly cadence, build master project tracker, stabilize calendar/inbox, publish KPI dashboard v1.

**Day 60:** Hit ?90% weekly completion, reduce CX response time to target, 3 key hires in final stages, SOPs v1 live.

**Day 90:** Zero major bottlenecks, full on-time promo/launch execution, hiring funnel humming, dashboards automated.



---



## Compensation



Competitive salary + performance bonus; benefits commensurate with experience and location.



## How to Apply



Submit your resume and a brief note sharing 1) an issue or operations turnaround you led, 2) your favorite PM stack, and 3) your earliest start date and salary expecations. 4) example of CEO tasks u handled and your top skill set.

NON SUBMISSION OF THIS WILL NOT BE CONSIDERED!

---



### Optional Add-Ons (use in your LinkedIn post/application form)



**Screening Questions**



1. Share a link/screenshot of a project dashboard you built (redact sensitive info).

2. Describe a time you reduced time-to-ship by changing the operating cadence.

3. Which e-co
trics do you track daily vs. weekly, and why?

4. In 5 bullets, outline your method to triage a CEO’s inbox/calendar.



**Must-Have Work Samples**



* Example of a weekly exec brief (1 page).

* A hiring tracker you’ve maintained.

* A standard operating procedure (SOP) you authored.

title: Customer Experience Professional Superstar
description:
Job Overview:



We are looking for a detail-oriented and proactive Customer Experience Professional Superstars this role will emphasize in building and maintaining strong long term relationships with our customers. The ideal candidate will possess excellent communication skills, a positive attitude and a passion for ensuring customer satisfaction while fostering loyalty and trust.



Key Responsibilities:

* Talk to customers everyday: Answer messages,
s, or calls from customers. Helping them with questions about orders, product and services

* Solve customer problems: Customer issues such as wrong items, delay, and complaint. Find out what happened and find a resolution quickly. Keep the customer updated until the issue is resolved.

* Follow-up with customers: Check in with customers after purchase to make sure they are happy. Ask for feedback and note any concerns

* Keep customers satisfied: Handle complaints calmly and professionally. Offer the best solution to make the customer happy

* Collect and share feedback: Record customers comments. Complaints and suggestions. Report common issues or trend to the team so improvements can be made

* Work with other teams: Coordinate with the logistics team, warehouse and sales team when needed

* Update Records: Log all customer interactions, issues, and actions taken in the CRM



About you:



* Can work independently with little supervision and learn new tools quickly

* Pays close attention to details and can identify the root cause of issues

* Stays calm and makes good decisions under pressure

* Manages time well and can handle multiple customers at once

* Flexible and open to changes in tasks, tools, or priorities

* Communicates clearly and confidently, including giving feedback to managers

* Experience with Australian furniture companies is a strong advantage

* Solid background in the e-commerce and retail industry



Qualifications:



* Proven experience in customer service, relationship management, or a similar role

* Excellent communication skills (both verbal and written)

* Strong interpersonal skills, empathy and intelligence, with an ability to build rapport with customers and internal teams

* Ability to handle difficult or sensitive situations with tact and professionalism

* Problem-solving skills and the ability to think critically and creatively

* Familiarity with CRM systems and other customer service software

* Strong organizational skills and attention to detail

* Ability to work independently and as part of a team





What we offer



* Salary offered: Php 40,000 - 45,000/month (Flexibility to increase for Genuine Superstars)

* “13th month” performance based bonus paid in December

* Annual performance and salary review every June

* HMO with an additional dependent

* 10 days Vacation leave

* 5 days Sick leave

* 3 days additional PTO (Christmas day, New Year’s day, & Good Friday)

* Full-time day shift (9:00 am to 6:00 pm - Australian Eastern Daylight Time (AEDT))

* Permanent work from home set-up





Home office requirements:

iCore 3 or it's equivalent with 8GB Ram

Internet speed should a minimum and stable 50mbps





ONLY SHORT-LISTED CANDIDATES WILL BE CONTACTED

title: Social Media Manager
description:
Social Media Manager & Content Creator

Motive — Texas CST (Remote / Async)

Part-Time | ~20 hrs/week



ABOUT THE ROLE:

Motive is a creative marketing agency based in the Dallas-Fort Worth area. We work exclusively with high-profile clients: nationally recognized speakers, authors, and brands producing premium video, social content, podcasts, ads, and websites.



We've produced a lot of work we're proud of. High-caliber shoots, major events, well-known names. And almost none of it is showing up on our own channels. That changes now.



We're hiring one person to own our social media presence end-to-end. Not to report to someone who manages it. To run it. You'll build our brand voice, grow our audiences, turn our portfolio of high-profile work into a feed that stops people mid-scroll, and actively work that audience to surface real business opportunities.



This is a high-autonomy, part-time role. You won't be handed a content calendar every week. You'll build it. If you thrive with clear goals and creative freedom, this is for you. If you need heavy direction and constant approval loops, it's probably not the right fit.



HOURS & SCHEDULE:

This is a part-time position of roughly 20 hours per week. Most of the work is async, but availability matters here more than most part-time roles. When we publish, we need you present. We expect active engagement within the first 30 minutes of every post going live, responding to comments, starting conversations, and feeding the algorithm when it matters most.



We expect a consistent posting cadence of 3 to 5 times per week across our platforms, with content planned and scheduled in advance.



At the end of every month, you will submit a 30-day content strategy for the upcoming month for our team to review and approve before the month begins. This keeps us aligned, gives you creative runway, and ensures nothing goes out without intention behind it.



Please include your expected part-time monthly rate when you apply.



PLATFORMS YOU'LL MANAGE:

Instagram, LinkedIn,
, TikTok, Pinterest, Threads, X 
)

TikTok will be a new build from scratch. LinkedIn is a top priority. We want to grow our presence there significantly and position ourselves as the go-to agency for high-profile personal brands.



WHAT YOU'LL OWN:

Build and manage a content calendar that keeps us posting 3 to 5 times per week, consistently.

Reformat and repurpose our existing photo and video library for every platform's native ratios (9:16, 1:1, 4:5, 16:9) so nothing looks like an afterthought.



Curate and showcase our high-profile client work in a way that reflects the caliber of what we do. The feed should feel like a portfolio, not just a feed.



Source and apply trending audio to Reels and TikToks. You know how to find what's actually gaining traction and pair it with content in a way that feels intentional, not forced.



Be present and actively engaging within the first 30 minutes of every post going live. Reply to early comments, spark conversation, and maximize that initial algorithm window.



Monitor comments and DMs for potential leads. Flag warm prospects, buying signals, and partnership opportunities so nothing falls through the cracks.



Create original shareable content including thought leadership, industry insights, and behind-the-scenes glimpses that build credibility.



Lead the growth of our LinkedIn presence as a strategic priority.

Grow our Social/Instagram following organically. No bots, no paid follower tactics, real audience growth.

Build our TikTok from the ground up with a clear channel strategy.

Design platform-native graphics and edit short-form video with the polish our brand demands.

Track performance metrics and bring weekly insights on what's working and what to adjust.



A note on lead identification: you don't need to be a salesperson. But you do need good instincts for when someone in our comments or DMs is more than just a follower, and know to flag it. We'll take it from there.



WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR:



Skills (non-negotiable):

Strong graphic design. You can produce polished, on-brand visuals from scratch.

Video editing for short-form content including Reels, TikToks, and YouTube Shorts. You know how to make a clip feel native to the platform it's going on.

Photo and video formatting for social ratios. You understand aspect ratios, crop strategy, and how to make existing media look intentional rather than just resized.

Trending audio awareness. You actively follow what's gaining traction on Instagram and TikTok and know how to use it effectively.

Social media strategy. You understand platform differences and know how to grow audiences with intention.

Community engagement. You're comfortable representing a brand in comments and DMs and know the difference between a conversation and a sales opportunity.

Excellent written English. Captions, hooks, replies, and copy that sound human and sharp.

Proficiency in tools like Adobe Suite, CapCut or equivalent.



Character traits that matter here:

You're a self-starter who sets your own agenda and hits it.

You take creative ownership and bring ideas. You don't wait to be told what to post.

You have an eye for quality and you feel it when something is off.

You're reliably available when posts go live. This isn't a set-it-and-forget-it role.

You communicate proactively and flag problems early instead of quietly missing deadlines.

You can work comfortably within a faith-informed company culture.



NICE TO HAVE:

Experience managing social media for high-profile personal brands, speakers, authors, or public figures.

We prefer all posting/scheduling done manually but will accept SproutSocial skills.

Experience growing a 
pany page or personal brand from a small following.

An eye for how to make premium work look premium on social. Not just competent, but elevated.

Comfort working with a small, lean team in a fast-moving creative environment.



HOW TO APPLY:

Please submit all of the following or your application will not be reviewed:

Links to social media accounts you currently manage or have managed.

2 to 3 examples of graphics or short-form videos you've created. Bonus points if they use trending audio or showcase reformatted media.

A brief note (3 to 5 sentences) on how you'd approach growing a LinkedIn page for a creative agency serving high-profile clients.

Your expected monthly rate for part-time hours (~20 hrs/week).

Applications without portfolio samples will not be considered. We want to see your actual work, not just a résumé.



A FEW MORE THINGS:

We're not looking for someone to punch the clock. We're looking for someone who sees our platforms as a canvas, understands that the work we've done deserves to be seen, wants to be the one to show it off, and stays sharp enough to spot when a comment might turn into a client.

Motive is a faith-based company. We don't require shared beliefs, but our culture and values are shaped by a Christian foundation, and we want to be upfront about that.



If you read this post in its entirety,
 
 with the subject line "I read the whole thing and would love to be considered."

title: VIrtual Assistant
description:
ABOUT THE ROLE



We are a growing mental health practice based in California, looking for a reliable, professional, and proactive

part-time Virtual Assistant to help us manage and grow our business. This is a long-term opportunity with significant

room to grow as our practice expands — including the potential to transition to a full-time role for the right candidate.

Our practice has an established brand identity, aesthetic, and tone of voice that is central to how we connect with our

community. The right candidate will take time to genuinely understand and embrace this identity. All content and

outreach materials will go through an approval process before publishing — this is standard practice for maintaining

our brand and is not micromanagement. We value a collaborative relationship where feedback flows both ways and

quality is always the priority.



You will be the first point of contact for prospective clients and referral partners, and will play a key role in how our

practice is perceived. We are looking for someone who is growth-oriented, takes pride in their work, and wants to

build a long-term professional relationship with a practice they genuinely care about.





SCHEDULE



Days: Monday to Friday

Hours: 4 hours per day — 9:00 AM to 1:00 PM Pacific Standard Time

Note: This is a graveyard shift position for Philippines-based applicants

Availability: Must be punctual and available during all scheduled hours

Growth: Hours may increase as the practice grows





KEY RESPONSIBILITIES



Phone & Client Inquiries

• Answer incoming calls professionally on behalf of the practice

• Make outbound calls to follow up with prospective clients

• Handle inquiries with warmth, empathy, and professionalism

• Send scheduling links and appointment confirmations

• Follow provided scripts and templates at all times

• Flag anything requiring the owner's direct attention immediately



 Management

• Monitor and respond to the practice's general inquiry inbox

• Draft responses using approved templates

• Maintain a clean, organized inbox

• Flag sensitive or complex inquiries for owner review before responding



Social Media Management

• Schedule and post content across
, Instagram, and LinkedIn

• Engage with followers per brand guidelines

• Maintain a consistent, professional, and on-brand voice at all times

• Submit all content drafts for owner approval before publishing

• Report on basic engagement metrics weekly



 Marketing

• Assist with building and sending
 campaigns via Mailchimp or similar

• Help grow and maintain the practice's
 list

• Format newsletters and promotional
s per provided templates

• Submit all
 drafts for owner review before sending



Professional Networking & Referral Outreach

• Conduct outreach to medical offices, primary care, pediatric offices, and OB/GYN practices

• Build and maintain relationships with medical office managers and their assistants

• Share practice resources and referral information professionally

• Maintain an organized referral contact database (Google Sheets, Notion, or other CRM)

• Follow up consistently with referral partners to nurture relationships

• Research and identify new potential referral sources in the local area

• Coordinate the sending of welcome packets or resource kits to referral partners





REQUIREMENTS



• Minimum 2 years virtual assistant, BPO, or call center experience

• Prior experience working US Pacific or West Coast hours

• Excellent spoken and written English — clear, warm, and professional phone manner is essential

• Experience managing social media accounts for a business

• Familiarity with tools like Slack, Notion, Canva, Buffer or Later, Mailchimp, and Google Workspace

• Experience with B2B outreach or professional networking, particularly within medical or healthcare

settings

• Professional, confident phone and
 communication style when reaching out to medical professionals

• Reliable high-speed internet connection (minimum 25 Mbps)

• Quiet, professional home workspace for phone calls 

• Own laptop or desktop computer

• Must be willing to sign a Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA) before starting

• Comfortable working in a mental health or healthcare-adjacent environment

• Brand-conscious and aesthetically aware — must learn and consistently apply the practice's visual

identity, tone, and voice

• Open to feedback and revision — all content requires owner review before publishing or sending

• Collaborative working style — comfortable submitting drafts and iterating until standards are met

• Not easily discouraged by revisions — understands feedback is about brand integrity, not work quality





NICE TO HAVE



• HIPAA certification is a plus but not required — certified candidates will be given preference

• Willingness to become HIPAA certified — the practice will cover this cost when the opportunity arises

• Experience supporting a healthcare, therapy, or wellness practice

• Experience creating Loom video walkthroughs for process documentation and team onboarding — or

strong willingness to learn. This will be an expected part of the role as the practice grows.

• Familiarity with Calendly or similar scheduling tools

• Experience with OpenPhone, Grasshopper, or virtual phone systems

• Familiarity with networking within medical or healthcare professional communities

• Low ego and psychologically aware — have strong interpersonal skills needed in creating relationships, given the multicultural makeup of the client and partner base





WHAT WE OFFER



• Part-time salary: $200–$300/month based on experience

• Stable, long-term position with a growing practice

• Consistent Monday–Friday schedule

• Supportive, respectful, and collaborative working relationship

• Clear growth path — potential to transition to full-time with expanded responsibilities, including HIPAA-compliant

client management

• HIPAA certification will be covered by the practice when the opportunity arises





HOW TO APPLY



Please apply with all of the following:

1. Your updated resume or CV

2. A brief written introduction or short video (2–3 minutes) telling us about your VA experience and why you are a

good fit for a mental health practice

3. Your specific experience working US Pacific Time or graveyard shift hours

4. Two professional references with contact information

5. One example of social media content or outreach material you have created for a previous employer or client

?? READ THIS FIRST — DO NOT APPLY IF:

You have never written or contributed to a Scope of Work document

You have never managed contractor follow-up (draw requests, punch lists, schedule accountability)

You cannot commit to a full-time, long-term role (this is not a side gig or freelance project)

You are currently juggling other part-time clients you are unwilling to drop



We are hiring one person to run our rehab operation. This role requires direct, hands-on construction operations experience — not general VA experience, not "I'm a fast learner," not "I managed projects in a different industry." If you check every box above as "yes, I've done this," keep reading.



About the Role

Accountable Home Buyers Inc. is a Chicago-area residential real estate investment firm running 6–10 simultaneous gut rehabs across the south Cook County suburbs. We're hiring a remote Project Manager to own the operational backbone of our rehab pipeline: contractor sourcing and vetting, scope of work production, material procurement coordination, permit follow-up, and field-team support.

This is not a task-executor role. You will be running a seven-figure construction operation remotely, working directly with the owner, a U.S.-based field coordinator, and Spanish-speaking contractor crews. You will be given full access to our templates, systems, and SOPs — your job is to execute them precisely, catch problems early, and keep projects moving.

Schedule: 30–40 hours per week, Monday–Friday, full-time commitment. Core overlap hours of 9 AM–2 PM Central Time (U.S.). Occasional flexibility required for contractor calls.

What You'll Own



Producing detailed bilingual (English/Spanish) Scopes of Work from property photos, 3D tours, and inspection reports

Building material purchase lists by cross-referencing our Contractors Guide and Measurements Document (Home Depot, Menards, Amazon)

Outbound contractor outreach — calling 20–50 licensed GCs per week to source, screen, and onboard bidders

Full contractor vetting: COI, W9, workers' comp, license verification, village registration, references, prior-work photos

Drafting and tracking contractor agreements using our 7-draw payment template

Permit coordination with south suburban village building departments (Dolton, Sauk Village, Hazel Crest, Calumet City, Richton Park, South Holland, Lynwood, Maywood, others)

Daily contractor check-ins, draw request review, and escalation of field issues

Maintaining CompanyCam photo documentation standards and project trackers

Managing communication between the owner, field rep, attorneys, and contractor crews



Required Experience (All of These — Non-Negotiable)



3+ years direct experience in construction project management, construction administration, or rehab/renovation operations

Hands-on experience hiring and managing contractors (GCs, subs, or trade-specific crews)

Direct experience purchasing construction materials and building material lists from supplier catalogs (Home Depot, Lowe's, Menards, or local equivalent)

Experience writing or contributing to line-item Scopes of Work

Experience coordinating with municipal building departments on permits, inspections, and code compliance

Fully bilingual English/Spanish — professional business writing in both languages (non-negotiable — you will be drafting legal-adjacent documents and translating trade scopes for Spanish-speaking crews)

Comfortable making 20+ outbound calls per day to U.S. contractors

Strong Google Workspace (Sheets, Drive, Docs) and comfort with tools like CompanyCam, Asana, DocuSign, DotLoop

Reliable high-speed internet, quiet workspace, U.S.-quality headset

Based in Latin America (Colombia, Mexico, Argentina, Peru, Costa Rica, Uruguay preferred for timezone alignment)



Nice to Have



Prior experience supporting a U.S. real estate investor, GC, or property management company

Familiarity with Cook County or Illinois municipal permitting

Experience reading architectural drawings or inspection reports

Paralegal or contracts background





Application Requirements (Incomplete Applications Will Be Rejected)

To be considered, you must submit all three of the following:

1. Résumé / CV

In English. Must clearly show the specific construction and contractor management experience listed in the required experience section above. Generic VA résumés without construction-specific roles will be rejected without response.

2. Video Introduction (Spoken in English)

A 3–5 minute video of yourself, on camera, speaking in English, covering:



1. **Required Response: Application Requirements**

   **Incomplete applications will be rejected.**



2. **To be considered, you must submit all three of the following:**



3. **Résumé / CV**

   In English. It must clearly show the specific construction and contractor management experience listed in the required experience section above. Generic VA résumés without construction-specific roles will be rejected without response.



4. **Video Introduction (spoken in English)**

   A 3–5 minute video of yourself, on camera, speaking in English, covering the following:



5. Who you are, where you are based, and whether working **8:00 AM–4:00 PM CST, Monday through Friday**, would be an issue now or in the foreseeable future.



6. Describe, from start to finish and to the best of your ability, how to build a garage to minimum code standard in a U.S. state.



7. Specific, concrete details about your **construction materials experience** — what materials you have purchased, from which suppliers, for what types of projects, and what your process looked like.

   Example: *“I managed material takeoffs for 12 single-family rehabs in 2024, sourcing from Home Depot Pro and local lumber yards, building purchase lists averaging $15,000–$40,000 per project.”*



8. Specific, concrete details about your **construction management experience** — how many contractors you have hired, how you vetted them, how you managed draws and schedules, and how you handled performance issues.



9. Your experience dealing with **building departments** — which municipalities, what permits, and what inspection processes you worked through.



10. Why you are ready to commit to a **full-time, long-term role**, and what you would need in order to leave your current situation.



Upload the video to YouTube (unlisted), Loom, or Google Drive and include the link in your application. Videos that are scripted, read from a teleprompter, or appear AI-generated will be rejected. We want to hear you speak naturally in English because you will be on calls with U.S. contractors, attorneys, and inspectors daily.

3. Written Response (150–300 words)

Answer this in English: "Describe the most difficult contractor or construction project situation you personally managed, what went wrong, how you handled it, and what you learned."

title: ClickUp Systems Architect & Automation Specialist (Agency Operations)
description:
We are looking for an experienced ClickUp Systems Architect to help us consolidate and systemise operations for a growing web design and digital marketing agency.



At present, our infrastructure is spread across tools such as Slack, Google Drive, Google Sheets, and documented SOPs. Part of this engagement will involve reducing tool sprawl by moving communication, workflows, and task management into ClickUp wherever appropriate.



Certain platforms — such as Go High Level (GHL) — will remain core systems and must be integrated cleanly into the overall workflow.



This role is about designing ClickUp as the central operating system, not simply maintaining an existing setup.



What You’ll Be Working With



Existing SOPs and partial documentation



Slack-based communication and handovers (to be reduced or replaced)



Google Drive and Google Sheets used for tracking and reporting



Go High Level (GHL) as a core CRM and automation platform



A team delivering web design, SEO, ads, and content services



You will work closely with the agency owner and key tea
mbers to:



Map how work currently happens



Identify what should move into ClickUp



D
 what should remain external (e.g. GHL)



Build a cleaner, more scalable operating system



Key Responsibilities

Process Mapping & Tool Consolidation



Work with leadership and tea
mbers to map existing workflows



Identify inefficiencies, duplication, and tool overlap



Design a ClickUp-first operating model where appropriate



Reduce reliance on Slack by shifting workflows, updates, and approvals into ClickUp



ClickUp Architecture & Build



Design the overall ClickUp workspace structure (Spaces, Folders, Lists)



Build workflows for different service lines (web, SEO, ads, content)



Create status flows that reflect real delivery stages



Build task templates, dependencies, and custom fields



Automation & Integration



Build advanced ClickUp automations



Integrate ClickUp with Go High Level (GHL) where required



Use n8n to connect systems where native automations are insufficient



Reduce manual handoffs between tools



Documentation & Enablement



Document systems, workflows, and logic



Create Loom walkthroughs or written SOPs



Support internal handover and adoption



Required Experience (Non-Negotiable)



You must have:



Deep experience designing and building ClickUp for agencies or service-based businesses



Strong understanding of ClickUp architecture and advanced automations



Experience consolidating workflows across multiple tools



Ability to work from incomplete or evolving process documentation



Confidence explaining why certain tools should be replaced, retained, or integrated



Highly Desirable (Bonus Skills)



Experience with n8n or similar automation tools



Experience integrating ClickUp with CRM platforms such as Go High Level



Background working with digital marketing or web agencies



Strong systems-thinking and documentation skills



Engagement Type



Project-based engagement to start



Potential for ongoing optimisation and systems ownership



Scope, timeline, and budget to be agreed



How to Apply (Important)



To apply, please include:



A short overview of your ClickUp experience



Examples of ClickUp systems you’ve built (screenshots or Looms)



An example of a workflow where you replaced or consolidated tools



Any experience with n8n or advanced integrations



Your availability and preferred engagement structure



Include the word “SYSTEMS” in the first line of your application.



Applications without examples will not be considered.

title: Design Lead
description:
Job Overview:



As the Design Lead, you'll be guiding the design team to build custom websites and graphics for clients. You'll shape and improve design processes while working closely with the SEO and web development teams. You'll also manage client communications to get design approvals. This means talking with clients, understanding their feedback, and passing it on to the design team, or handling some design mockups/updates yourself. Since you'll be
ing clients daily, strong communication skills are required. 



Furthermore, we expect that you can:



- manage a design team 

- oversee and always be on top of the design projects (if you are not proactive, this is not for you!)

- improve existing design processes to streamline web design and development

- identify and incorporate the best designs and processes into our system so that we can continuously improve output and quality

- developing and maintaining design libraries (not pretty designs, but instead serve a purpose for our SEO clients)

- communicate with clients and understand their needs

- Communicate client business goals to the dev team to make sure that designs have a business impact on clients

- collaborate with the SEO team to back up their campaigns

- Do quality checking of the live URL before and after launch



Job Requirements:



1. A Paypal account (we pay through Paypal).

2. At least 2+ years of experience in a design role with at least one year of leadership experience and team management.

3. Advance-level knowledge of the software Figma; Proficiency in other design tools such as Adobe Creative Suite and Sketch is a plus

4. Knowledge/experience and a thorough understanding of designing (or building) several WordPress (or any CMS) websites.

5. Has experience using project management software such as ClickUp, Asana, Airtable, or similar tools.

6. Strong portfolio showcasing a variety of design projects and styles

7. A deep understanding of design principles, user-centered design, user experience (UX) design, and the ability to look for those and translate them into design/development.

8. Exceptional communication skills with solid English proficiency, both written and verbal

9. Demonstrates understanding of SEO requirements in design decisions

10. With a keen eye for details and quality control



If you are confident that you possess the design wizardry and leadership skills we are looking for, we invite you to complete the form below to express your interest in joining our team.







Only those who follow instructions will be considered for screening. Thank you
